name: Abbadon Wasp
description: A massive wasp with two pairs of spindly legs and long chitinous arms with the face of a tortured human.

features.name: Flight
features.description: While flying, the Wasp canmark a Stress to move up to Far range instead of Close range before taking an action.

features.name: Terrifying
features.description: When the Wasp makes a successful attack all PCs within Close range lose a Hope and you gain a Fear.

features.name: Stinging Strikes
features.description: Mark a Stressto fly into a rage and make an attack against all targets in Close range. Targets the Abbadon Wasp succeeds against take3d8 physical damage and you gain a Fear for each PC that marks HP.

features.name: Enough!
features.description: If the Wasp takes Major damage, move the Wasp to a point within Close range and make a standard attack against a target.
features.trigger: If the Wasp takes Major damage

features.name: Swatting Pests
features.description: If the Wasp is attacked by a flying PC within Far rangespend a Fearto move into Very Close range with them and make a standard attack with advantage. On a success, the PC marks a Stress.
features.trigger: If the Wasp is attacked by a flying PC within Far rangespend a Fearto move into Very Close range with them and make a standard attack with advantage

name: Adult Flickerfly
description: Un insecte massif aux écailles iridescentes et aux ailes cristallines se déplaçant plus vite que l’œil ne peut suivre.

features.name: Relentless (3)
features.description: La Luciole peut être mise sous le projecteur jusqu’à trois fois par tour du MJ. Dépensez une Peur comme d’habitude pour la placer sous le projecteur.

features.name: Never Misses
features.description: Quand la Luciole fait une attaque, l’Évasion de la cible est divisée par deux contre cette attaque.

features.name: Deadly Flight
features.description: En vol, la Luciole peut se déplacer jusqu’à Portée Loin au lieu de Portée Proche avant d’agir.

features.name: Whirlwind
features.description: Tourbillonnez, faisant une attaque contre toutes les cibles à Portée Très Proche. Les cibles touchées subissent 3d8 dégâts physiques directs.

features.name: Mind Dance
features.description: Créez un spectacle magiquement éblouissant qui emprisonne l’esprit des ennemis proches. Toutes les cibles à Portée Proche doivent faire un jet de Réaction d’Instinct. Pour chaque cible qui échoue, vous gagnez une Peur et la Luciole apprend une des peurs de la cible.

features.name: Hallucinatory Breath
features.description: Compte à rebours (Boucle 1d6). Quand la Luciole subit des dégâts pour la première fois, activez le compte à rebours. Quand il se déclenche, la Luciole souffle un gaz hallucinatoire sur toutes les cibles devant elle jusqu’à Portée Loin. Les cibles doivent faire un jet de Réaction d’Instinct ou être tourmentées par des hallucinations terrifiantes. Les cibles dont les peurs sont connues de la Luciole ont un désavantage. Les cibles qui échouent perdent 2 Espoirs et subissent 3d8+3 dégâts magiques directs.
features.trigger: Quand la luciole subit des dégâts pour la première fois, activez le compte à rebours

features.name: Uncanny Reflexes
features.description: Quand la Luciole subit des dégâts d’une attaque à Portée Proche, subissez la moitié des dégâts.
features.trigger: Quand la luciole subit des dégâts d’une attaque à portée proche

name: Aithon
description: A massive, rust-colored horse creature with a bushy mane used as a beast of burden by god-like entities.

features.name: Wild Horses
features.description: The Aithon cannot beRestrained.

features.name: Whirling Kicks
features.description: Spend a Fearto make an attack against all targets within Close range. Targets the Aithon succeeds against take3d12+10physical damage and are knocked back to Far range.

features.name: Pummeling Leap
features.description: Mark a Stressand choose a target within Far range to leap to and make an attack. On a success, the target takes3d20+8physical damage and isRestrainedunder the Aithon’s hooves until they succeed on a Strength roll or the Aithon moves.

features.name: Tail Swat
features.description: When the Aithon is attacked from behind within Very Close range, you canmark a Stressto swat the attacker for3d8+2physical damage.
features.trigger: When the Aithon is attacked from behind within Very Close range

name: Amphisbaena
description: A flying two-headed lizard that spits burning fire and freezing ice.

features.name: Two-Headed
features.description: When the Amphisbaenae has marked half or more of their HP, choose either the “Spit Fire” or “Ice Bomb”. The Amphisbaenae can no longer use the chosen feature.

features.name: Flying
features.description: While flying, the Amphisbaena gains a +3 bonus to their Difficulty.

features.name: Breath Weapon
features.description: You mustspend a Fearto use the Amphisbaena’s standard attack, but they attack all targets within Very Close range.

features.name: Spit Fire
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Far range. On a success the target takes3d4+4magic damage and isIgniteduntil they’re extinguished with a successful Finesse Roll. WhileIgnited, the target takes 1d4magic damage when the Amphisbaena is spotlighted the first time on a GM turn. If it has not done so, the Amphisbaena canmark a Stressto take the spotlight again to use its “Ice Bomb” action this GM turn.

features.name: Ice Bomb
features.description: Choose a point within Far range. All targets within Melee range of that point must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll or take3d4+4magic damage. If it has not done so, the Amphisbaena canmark a Stressto take the spotlight again to use its “Spit Fire” action this GM turn.

name: Angel
description: A beautiful being of solid light that acts as messenger of a God. Motives & Tactics: Travel to beleaguered, bring hope, dispel darkness

features.name: Flying
features.description: While flying, the Angel gains a +2 bonus to their Difficulty.

features.name: Prayer of Healing
features.description: Mark a number of Stress and clear HP equal to this number from the Angel or an ally within Close range.

features.name: Light Them Up
features.description: When you succeed on a standard attack against a target, you can mark a Stress to also deal damage to a second target the attack succeeds against behind the first and within Far range.
features.trigger: When you succeed on a standard attack against a target

features.name: Dazzling Light
features.description: When the Angel takes Major or greater damage, they release a flash of bright light. All creatures within Very Close range must succeed on an Instinct Reaction Roll or become blinded, making them Vulnerable until they next act.
features.trigger: When the Angel takes Major or greater damage

name: Anger Scourged
description: A person overcome with anger, surrounded by anger spirits in the form of floating spectral skulls.

features.name: Cloaking Spirits
features.description: Increase the Scourged's Difficulty against attacks by the number of unmarked Stress.

features.name: Inner Rage
features.description: The Scourged has a critical success on an action roll of 19 or 20.

features.name: Counter Emotion
features.description: A PC can make a Presence Roll to display calming behavior. On a success, mark 1d4 Stress.

features.name: Infectious Rage
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Very Close range. On a success, the target becomes Enraged. While Enraged, the target must make a Presence Reaction Roll when they act. On a success, they end the condition. On a failure, they must mark a Stress and attack the nearest creature. Fear Scourged Someone who experienced an acute or chronic frightful experience can become a Fear Scourged. Sorrow Scourged A Sorrow Scourged has likely experienced a recent traumatic event or loss of a loved one.

name: Ankylosaurus
description: An armored quadrupedal dinosaur with a clubbed tai!.

features.name: Armored
features.description: When the Dinosaur takes damage, reduce it by 1d8.

features.name: Tail Sweep
features.description: Make an attack against all targets within Very Close Range. Targets the Dinosaur succeeds against are knocked down, becoming Vulnerable until they next act.

features.name: Primeval Instinct
features.description: When the Dinosaur makes a successful standard attack against a Vulnerable target, you can mark a Stress to deal double damage.
features.trigger: When the Dinosaur makes a successful standard attack against a Vulnerable target

name: Aquatic Basilisk
description: An aquatic creature with blue stripes, fins and a deadly gaze.

features.name: Easy Pickings
features.description: Targets of the Basilisk's attacks must state if they are looking at the Basilisk. The Basilisk has advantage against targets who avoid looking at the Basilisk.

features.name: Acid Breath
features.description: Mark a Stress to cover the area within Very Close range in front of the Basilisk in acid. All targets within the area take 2d8+5 magic damage when they act. Targets with an active calcification countdown take double damage.

features.name: Calcifying Gaze
features.description: When the Basilisk takes damage from an attacker within Very Close range, or makes a standard attack against a target that is looking at them, you can spend a Fear to force the target to make an Instinct Reaction Roll. On a failure, they begin to turn to stone, marking a HP and starting a Calcification Countdown (4). The countdown ticks down when the Basilisk is attacked. When it triggers, the target must make a death move. If the Basilisk is defeated, all calcification countdowns end.
features.trigger: When the Basilisk takes damage from an attacker within Very Close range

name: Ashen Fawn
description: A lanky deer-like creature with a mottled coat whose body is beginning to ignite like their sire.

features.name: Minion (8)
features.description: The Ashen Fawn is defeated when they take any damage. For every 8 damage a PC deals to the Ashen Fawn, defeat an additional Minion within range the attack would succeed against.

features.name: Scatter
features.description: When a PC moves within Close range of the Ashen Fawn, it bolts in a random direction to Far range, leaving behind a cloud of ash and soot encompassing Very Close range. Creatures within the cloud have disadvantage on attack rolls until they move out of it, after which the cloud dissipates.
features.trigger: When a PC moves within Close range of the Ashen Fawn

name: Ashen Stag
description: A towering quadruped beast with flaming antlers and molten scars. Its body emits searing heat, and its howl curdles courage.

features.name: Ashen Trail
features.description: When the Ashen Stag makes an action roll, all targets within Far range mark a Stress as smoke fills their lungs.

features.name: Domineering Howl
features.description: When the Ashen Stag enters the scene,spend a Fearto emit a roar of elemental rage that causes a wave of fear. All targets within Far range must succeed on an Instinct Reaction Roll (19). Targets who fail become temporarilyVulnerableand mark 2 Stress. WhileVulnerablea target must mark a Stress when they make an action roll. Targets who succeed mark a Stress.

features.name: Impact Stomp
features.description: Mark a Stressto make an attack against all targets within Very Close Range. Targets the Ashen Stag succeeds against take3d8+3physical damage, are knocked back to Close range, and you gain a Fear.

features.name: Molten Core
features.description: When the Ashen stag takes Major damage, roll ad12. On a 6 or higher, all targets within Close range must succeed on a Strength Reaction Roll (18) or mark a HP.
features.trigger: When the Ashen stag takes Major damage

name: Baihu Dragon Turtle
description: A tiger-like humanoid watching over a jungle.

features.name: Beastly Power
features.description: When you initially roll damage, if you roll the maximum value on a die, also roll a new die of that type and add it to the total.

features.name: Call of the Jungle
features.description: Once per scene, mark a Stress to summon a Tiger, who appears at Close range and immediately takes the spotlight.

features.name: Thorny Cage
features.description: Spend a Fear to form a cage around a target and Restrain them until they're freed with a successful Strength Roll (15). When a creature makes an action roll against the cage, they must mark a Stress.

features.name: Nine Lives
features.description: When the Baihu would mark their last HP and has unmarked Stress, mark all remaining Stress to remain on 1 HP, becoming Vulnerable. Immediately spotlight the Baihu.
features.trigger: When the Baihu would mark their last HP and has unmarked Stress

features.name: Nature's Karma
features.description: When a creature within Far range uses an area of effect attack or otherwise harms the environment, deal 2dlO magic damage to the attacker and you gain a Fear.
features.trigger: When a creature within Far range uses an area of effect attack or otherwise harms the environment

name: Belligerent Sister
description: A raider that lives for the kill, dressed in scavenged armor, a vile-looking mask and dirty clothes.

features.name: Thug
features.description: The Belligerent Sister can mark a Stress to add 1d8 damage to a successful attack.

features.name: Deflection
features.description: When the Belligerent Sister marks 2 or fewer HP from an attack within Very Close range, roll a d8. On a 6 or higher, the damage is reduced by a threshold.
features.trigger: When the Belligerent Sister marks 2 or fewer HP from an attack within Very Close range

name: Blood Cleric
description: A cultist wearing blood-stained robes.

features.name: Blood Letting
features.description: Mark a Stress to summon a Blood Ooze from a pool of blood. You can mark a HP to use the Blood Cleric's own blood.

features.name: Blood Curse of Control
features.description: Mark a HP to force a target to make an Instinct Reaction Roll. On a failure, the target is Controlled by the Blood Cleric and must lose a Hope. While Controlled the target can't act except to make an Instinct Roll. On a success, clear the condition and the target becomes immune to being Controlled.

features.name: Puppetry
features.description: Spend a Fear to spotlight all Controlled targets and direct them to attack a target within range.

features.name: Blood Armor
features.description: When a creature within Very Close range takes Major or greater damage, you can mark a Stress to increase the Blood Cleric's thresholds by 2. Blood Hunter A Blood Hunter collects the blood of enemies and sacrifices some of their health to boost their abilities.
features.trigger: When a creature within Very Close range takes Major or greater damage

name: Blood Hunter
description: A cultist who uses blood magic to hunt foes.

features.name: Blood Vial
features.description: The Blood Hunter has advantage on attacks against targets whose blood is in their possession.

features.name: Draw Blood
features.description: Once per scene, mark a HP then make a standard attack. The Blood Hunter's standard attacks deal 3dlO+3 physical and magic damage instead of their standard damage until the end of the scene.

features.name: Blood Curse
features.description: When the target of the Blood Hunter's attack marks HP, you can mark a Stress to move a random card from the PC's loadout into their vault until the end of the scene. Blood Mage A Blood Mage can magically manipulate or enhance blood to support their allies and draw blood from their enemies. Blood Zealot Blood Zealots love the sight of blood and happily enter a killing frenzy in the name of their cult.
features.trigger: When the target of the Blood Hunter's attack marks HP

name: Blood Mage
description: A cultist who has studied the power of blood magic.

features.name: Hemokinesis
features.description: Mark a Stress to telekinetically move blood from a fresh corpse or other source within Close range to another point within Close range. A creature crossing the blood must make an Agility Reaction Roll (12). On a failure, the creature falls down, becoming Vulnerable until they next act and ending their turn.

features.name: Rush of Blood
features.description: Mark a Stress to enhance the blood of an ally within Close range. When they are spotlighted and within Close range, they can mark a Stress to take the spotlight a second time that turn. This feature lasts until the Blood Mage takes Severe damage or takes a new action.

name: Blood Zealot
description: A cultist who enters a blood rage, often sacrificing much of their health to do so.

features.name: Blood Rage
features.description: Once per scene, mark up to 2 HP then make a standard attack against a target within range. The Blood Zealot enters a blood rage until the end of the scene. While in a rage, deal an additional IdlO magic damage per HP marked.

features.name: Attack of Opportunity
features.description: When a target leaves Melee range of the Blood Zealot, make a standard attack against them. D Devils Devils are a type of demon that were once angels, now fallen, banished to the Circles Below, and corrupted by them. Many Devils were created when the New Gods usurped power and cleared out the angels loyal to the Forgotten Gods. Devils embrace lives of sin, indulging in multiple sins unlike most demons who favor just one. In the Circles Below, they fight for power between themselves and other demons. They build societies around making slaves of the souls of the damned that flow into the Circles Below. Devil Dealmaker Some Devils use their charm to strike deals with others. They always keep to the terms of the deal, but add in loopholes for themselves and clauses to punish and profit from those who break their word.
features.trigger: When a target leaves Melee range of the Blood Zealot

name: Bloodborn
description: A vile creature of blood born from a dark ritual.

features.name: Reborn
features.description: The Bloodborn has access to features and experiences used by their former self (or selves). Mark a Stress to use them, in addition to any other requirements.

features.name: Your Life Is Mine
features.description: Spend a Fear to make an attack against a target within Melee range by lifting them off their feet by their neck. On a success deal 2d20 direct physical damage and the target is Restrained until they break free with a successful Strength or Finesse Roll. While Restrained, they must mark a Stress on every roll with Fear.

features.name: Bleed For Me
features.description: When a target of the Bloodborn's standard attack marks HP, you can mark a Stress to clear a HP. A Blood Cleric leads blood rituals and sacrifices some of their health to influence other life forms.
features.trigger: When a target of the Bloodborn's standard attack marks HP

name: Brutalon
description: A squat, tanky lizard with three flanged, mace-like bone clubs at the end of its tail, a long, flat horn and large scoop-like claws.

features.name: Defensive Stance
features.description: The Brutalon takes a defensive stance, becomingDug In. WhileDug In, all the Brutalon’s attacks have disadvantage and when they take damage, reduce it by 10. If the Brutalon moves, it clears theDug In condition.

features.name: Flip
features.description: Mark a Stressand make an attack against a target within Close range. If the Brutalon isDug In, it has advantage. On a success, the target is heaved into the air, falling at a point within Far range and taking2d20+5 direct physical damage.

features.name: Tail Flail
features.description: Spend a Fearto make an attack against all targets in a Very Close range behind the Brutalon. Targets the Brutalon succeeds against take3d10+3 physical damage and are knocked back to Far range.

name: Canid Bodyguard
description: A dog-like humanoid that is loyal to a master.

name: Captured Thrall
description: A humanoid with a nest of larvae on their back, using them as a source of food.

features.name: Minion (6)
features.description: The Thrall is defeated when they take any damage. For every 6 damage a PC deals to the Thrall, defeat an additional Minion within range the attack would succeed against.

features.name: Food Source
features.description: A member of the Hive within Melee range of a defeated Thrall can mark a Stress to clear 1 HP.

features.name: Group Attack
features.description: Spend a Fearto choose a target and spotlight all Thralls within Close range of them. Those Minions move into Melee range of the target and make one shared attack roll. On a success, they deal 7 physical damage each. Combine this damage.

name: Carnivorous Plant
description: A large plant with a mouth full of razor sharp teeth, surrounded by a mass of thick, flowering vines.

features.name: Plant
features.description: The Plant can't move.

features.name: Constrict
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Far range. On a success, mark a Stress to Constrict the target. While Constricted, the target is Restrained and Vulnerable until they break free with a successful Finesse or Strength Roll.

features.name: Engulf
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Melee range or a Constricted target. On a success, spend a Fear to Engulf the target within the mouth of the Plant, ending the Constricted condition, until the Plant takes Severe damage or the target breaks free with a successful Agility Roll (18). While Engulfed a target is Restrained and takes 1d2O+2 physical damage on each roll with Fear.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Plant makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear. Giant Fungus Giant Fungi are large mushrooms that grow in underground caves and in the Fae Realm, often near Fungril colonies. Holes on the underside of the cap can release spores. Kelp Forest Forests of Kelp found underwater can strangle those that try to harm them, and can grow to enormous sizes by absorbing weak magic.
features.trigger: When the Plant makes a successful attack against a PC

name: Chimera
description: A giant, fire-breathing monster with a lion's head, a goat's body, and a serpent's tail.

features.name: Rending Jaws
features.description: When the Chimera makes a successful attack, the target must mark 2 Armor Slots instead of 1 to reduce the damage by one threshold.

features.name: Breath of Fire
features.description: Spend a Fear to target up to three PCs in front of the Chimera within Close range. All targets must make an Agility Reaction Roll (20). Targets who fail take 3d12 magic damage. Targets who succeed must either mark an Armor Slot or mark a Stress.

features.name: Serpent Strike
features.description: When the Chimera is attacked by a PC within Very Close range, you can mark a Stress to make an attack against them. On a success, deal 4d4+10 physical damage and Poison them until their next rest or they succeed on a Knowledge Roll (20). While Poisoned, the target must roll a d6 before they make an action roll. On a result of 3 or lower, they must mark a Stress.
features.trigger: When the Chimera is attacked by a PC within Very Close range

features.name: Double Claw
features.description: After the Chimera makes a standard attack, you can mark a Stress to make an additional standard attack. If both attacks target the same creature, combine the damage.
features.trigger: After the Chimera makes a standard attack

name: Chosen Transformed
description: A divine warrior bestowed with more power than they can control.

features.name: Frightening Aura
features.description: When the Chosen is spotlighted, you gain a Fear if a PC is within Melee range.

features.name: Unstable
features.description: Attacks that deal magic damage to the Chosen trigger a wild magic effect (Introduction of this book).

features.name: Energy Surge
features.description: Spend a Fear to release energy out to Very Close range. All targets must make an Agility Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take 4d6+9 magic damage. Targets who succeed take half damage.

features.name: Smite
features.description: Twice per scene when you deal damage to a target, you can mark a Stress to double the damage dealt. Disfavored If a Chosen fails at their task, for example by being foiled by a plucky adventuring party, they can be punished by their God. A Disfavored becomes a monstrosity, with their features changing such as by growing extra limbs, gaining eyes and sharpened teeth, and growing a tail.

name: Chromatic Eel
description: A large astral eel whose coloration shifts based on its surroundings and mood.

features.name: Planar Phasing
features.description: The Eel exists on multiple planes at once making it resistant to physical damage.

features.name: Shocking Strike
features.description: Spend a Fearand choose a target within Far range. Move into Melee range of the target and make an attack with advantage. On a success, the target takes3d6+10physical damage and marks 2 stress.

features.name: Camouflage
features.description: Mark a Stressto give an attack against the Eel disadvantage.

name: Cilrathi Queensguard
description: A lithe faerie with chitinous skin and multiple arms, each wielding a weapon.

features.name: Ramp up
features.description: You mustspend a Fearto spotlight the Queensguard. While spotlighted, they can make their standard attack against all targets within range.

features.name: Precise Strike
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Very Close range. On a success, the target takes 3d8direct physical damage.

features.name: For the Queen
features.description: Mark all of the Queensguard’s remaining HP. An ally within Very Close range clears 2 HP and 2 Stress.

features.name: Whirling Blades
features.description: When the Queensguard is within Very Close range of a Wasp Queen who would take damage, you canmark a Stressto give the attack disadvantage. If the attack still succeeds, reduce the number of HP marked by 1.
features.trigger: When the Queensguard is within Very Close range of a Wasp Queen who would take damage

name: Cinder Ant
description: A dog-sized insect that smoulders with smoke and ash, its insides burning with divine magic.

features.name: Branding Bite
features.description: Make a standard attack against a target. On a success,spend a Fearto disarm the target. The Cinder Ant takes the item and moves to a point within Close range. It can be recovered with a successful Strength or Finesse action roll.

features.name: Ignition End
features.description: When the Cinder Ant is defeated, it explodes. All targets within Melee range must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll or mark 1 HP.
features.trigger: When the Cinder Ant is defeated

features.name: Burning Rage
features.description: When the Cinder Ant misses an attack, they canmark a Stressto do2d6+1physical damage to the target.
features.trigger: When the Cinder Ant misses an attack

name: Circles Below: Dis
description: A city in the Circles Below populated with demons.

features.name: City of Sin
features.description: The Demons that rule Dis are involved in political games for power. A PC can inquire about a person or event with a Presence Roll. What they learn depends on the outcome of their roll. •	Critical Success: Learn the location of the person or event and some relevant background information. •	Success with Hope: Learn of a gluttonous demon who can provide the information if they are fed their favorite food. •	Success with Fear: Learn where the information can be found through gambling. •	Any Failure: The demon responds poorly to the inquiries. The PC must mark a Stress and barter in exchange for minor information. Who are the major players in Dis and what vices do they embody?

features.name: Corrupting Influence
features.description: Once per scene, when a PC makes a sinful action (pride, greed, wrath, envy, lust, gluttony, or sloth), they gain a Sin token. These can be spent to gain advantage on an action roll while in Dis. How might they achieve each of these sins while in Dis?

features.name: Souls as Slaves
features.description: Souls of the damned have been stripped of their spirit, and dutifully attend to the menial tasks within Dis as slaves of the demon ruling class. Is buying and selling souls a commonplace occurrence? Do demons barter with souls?

features.name: Spirited Souls
features.description: A group of souls with a little remaining spirit attempt to make an escape from a cruel master in sight of the PCs, seeking the means to become demons themselves or escape to the Hallows Above. What crimes did the souls commit to be condemned to the Circles Below? Are they redeemable?

features.name: Suspicious Denizens
features.description: When the PCs don't have any Sin tokens, spend a Fear to have a Demon of Hubris, a Demon of Wrath, and a Demon of Jealousy investigate the PCs. What were the demons on their way to do?
features.trigger: When the PCs don't have any Sin tokens

name: Clank Juggernaut
description: A large clank with one sword arm and one magical cannon arm.

features.name: Internal Gearing
features.description: The Juggernaut's interior can be exposed with a Strength Roll (16). Attack rolls made against the interior have +2 Difficulty and the Clank must mark an additional HP on a success.

features.name: Pulse Cannon
features.description: Mark a Stress and make an attack against a target within Far range, dealing 3d10+5 magic damage on a success and knocking the target back to Far range.

features.name: Blade Spin
features.description: Spend a Fear to make an attack against all targets within Very Close range. Targets the Clank succeeds against take 2dlO physical damage.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Clank makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.
features.trigger: When the Clank makes a successful attack against a PC

name: Cloud Giant
description: A giant that rides atop a winged beast.

features.name: Deadly Flight
features.description: The Giant can move up to Far range instead of Close range before taking an action.

features.name: Deadly Companion
features.description: Once per scene, summon a Griffon, Wyvern, or similar Tier 2 or 3 flying animal under the Giant's control. The adversary appears at Close range and is immediately spotlighted.

features.name: Cloud Wall
features.description: Mark a Stress to create a wall of gaseous or solid cloud between two points within Far range. The cloud obscures vision. Solid cloud can be dispersed with a successful Strength Roll (12).

features.name: Mistform
features.description: When the Giant takes physical damage, you can spend a Fear to take half damage.
features.trigger: When the Giant takes physical damage

name: Congregation of the End
description: Hopeless masses given over to the doctrine of the end, welcoming the death of the world to end their suffering.

features.name: Horde (3d6+1)
features.description: When the Congregation has marked half or more of their HP, their standard attack deals3d6+1physical damage instead.

features.name: Death Comes for Us All
features.description: When this adversary makes a successful attack against a PC, mark a Stressto make the PCVulnerableuntil they next take the spotlight.
features.trigger: When this adversary makes a successful attack against a PC

name: Contract Killer
description: A veteran criminal and murderer working for a crime syndicate.

features.name: Terrifying
features.description: When the Killer makes a successful attack, all PCs within Far range lose a Hope and you gain a Fear.

features.name: Like a Scythe Through Wheat
features.description: The Killer may defeat2d4Minions or civilians within Close range. All allies of defeated characters within a Far range of the Killer must mark a Stress.

features.name: Just Another Face in the Crowd
features.description: Spend a Fearto reveal that the Killer was already in the scene as they throw off their surprise. Immediately make an attack with advantage against a target within range.

features.name: Gap in the Armor
features.description: When the Killer inflicts at least Major damage, you maymark a Stressto make it direct damage.
features.trigger: When the Killer inflicts at least Major damage

features.name: Uncanny Dodge
features.description: When an attack would hit the Killer, you canmark a Stressto force the attacker to re-roll and take the new result.
features.trigger: When an attack would hit the Killer

name: Corrupted Piranha School
description: A school of plate-sized carnivorous fish who have been twisted by dark magics.

features.name: Horde (3d4+2)
features.description: When the School has marked half or more of its HP, its standard attack deals3d4+2 physical damage instead.

features.name: Blood in the Water
features.description: If a target marks HP from another source within Close range of the School,mark a Stressto have the School move to Very Close and make a standard attack with advantage.
features.trigger: If a target marks HP from another source within Close range of the School

features.name: Leeches
features.description: The waters of the mire are infested with leeches. These creatures attach themselves to any flesh they can find. A PC who has exposed flesh in the water has several attach to them. They can make an Instinct Roll to remove the leeches. On a failure, the PC who has the leeches on their body marks a HP. On a success they mark a Stress. Do the leeches carry disease? What precautions can the PCs take to prevent more leeches?

features.name: No Rest for the Weary
features.description: If the PCs choose to rest in the Dread Mire, you gain an additional1d4Fear. What things do the PCs see or hear during their downtime to make it less restful? Can you advance a long-term countdown as a result of their delay?

features.name: You Belong to the Mire
features.description: Spend a Fearto introduce a sinking mire pit to the scene. All PCs must succeed on an Instinct Reaction Roll. On a failure, the PC isSinkingin the mire and a total of 3 Stress must be marked (either by theSinkingPC or others who can aid them) to clear the condition. If not enough Stress is available, PCs must mark HP instead. Are there vines or roots nearby to grab onto? What thoughts run through PCs’ minds as they are pulled under?

features.name: Piranha
features.description: When a PC marks HP while in water, you canspend a Fearto summon one Corrupted Piranha School for each PC. They appear at Far range and are immediately spotlighted. Are there places nearby to climb out of the water? Is the water deep enough to force some or all of the PCs to swim?
features.trigger: When a PC marks HP while in water

features.name: I See You
features.description: When a PC fails or rolls with Fear to gain information about the Dread Mire in “Sinister Tidings”, activate aConsequence Countdown (8). When the Countdown triggers, the PCs stumble onto the source of the corruption unaware. Resolve the current scene, then gain a Fear and shift to the Heart of the Mire environment and spotlight it. What lies in wait at the Heart of the Mire? What advantage might be gained over the surprised PCs?
features.trigger: When a PC fails or rolls with Fear to gain information about the Dread Mire in “Sinister Tidings”

name: Darkheart Sister
description: A dangerous humanoid that has accepted the shadows and become enveloped in rage.

features.name: Brutal
features.description: The Darkheart Sister rerolls any 1s when rolling damage.

features.name: Surging Strike
features.description: Mark a Stress to attack a target within Far range. On a success, deal 3d6+12 physical damage and the target must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll or become temporarily Restrained.

features.name: Lariat Throw
features.description: Spend a Fear to throw a target Restrained by the Darkheart Sister to a point within Far range. If a target is knocked into a solid object or another creature, both the target and the creature take 2d12 physical damage.

name: Deadeye Sister
description: A figure in leathers with a heavy crossbow and a masked helmet.

features.name: Bloody Advantage
features.description: When the Deadeye Sister attacks a target that has marked HP they can mark a Stress to gain advantage. If the target has marked half or more of their HP the target also marks a Stress if the attack succeeds.

features.name: Called Shot
features.description: Make an attack with a +0 modifier. If it still hits, the target takes 3d10+13 damage, and the target must mark 2 Armor Slots instead of one to reduce the damage. If they do not or cannot, they also mark an additional HP.

name: Death Wraith
description: A floating shadow being with wisps of smoke drifting off its body.

features.name: Incorporeal Form
features.description: The Wraith is resistant to physical damage. Mark a Stress to move up to Close range through solid objects.

features.name: Unravel Mind
features.description: Mark a Stress and make an attack against a target within Very Close range. On a success, the target marks 1d6 Stress. If they have marked all Stress, the target becomes Maddened until they clear all marked Stress. While Maddened they can only take short rest downtime moves during a long rest and they develop a flaw such as a compulsion, obsession, or phobia.

features.name: Foment Disorder
features.description: When you succeed on a standard attack against a PC, you can mark a Stress to switch two of the PC's traits until their next long rest.
features.trigger: When you succeed on a standard attack against a PC

name: Demon of Avarice
description: Une monstruosité royale encapuchonnée aux cornes circulaires ornées de trésors.

features.name: Money Talks
features.description: Les attaques contre le Démon sont faites avec désavantage à moins que l’attaquant ne dépense une poignée d’or. Ce Démon commence avec un nombre de poignées égal au nombre de PJ. Quand une cible coche des PV suite à l’attaque standard du Démon, elle peut dépenser une poignée d’or au lieu de cocher des PV. Ajoutez une poignée d’or au Démon pour chaque poignée dépensée par les PJ.

features.name: Numbers Must Go Up
features.description: Ajoutez un bonus aux jets d’attaque du Démon égal au nombre de poignées d’or qu’il possède.

features.name: Money Is Time
features.description: Dépensez 3 poignées d’or (ou une Peur) pour placer sous le projecteur 1d4+1 alliés.

name: Demon of Despair
description: Un démon-créature encapuchonné aux longs membres, suintant les ombres.

features.name: Depths of Despair
features.description: Le Démon inflige le double de dégâts aux PJ avec 0 Espoir.

features.name: Your Struggle Is Pointless
features.description: Accablez l’esprit de tous les PJ à Portée Loin. Toutes les cibles affectées remplacent leur Dé d’Espoir par un d8 jusqu’à ce qu’elles obtiennent un succès avec Espoir ou leur prochain repos.

features.name: Your Friends Will Fail You
features.description: Quand un PJ échoue avec Peur, faites perdre un Espoir à tous les autres PJ à Portée Proche.
features.trigger: Quand un pj échoue avec peur

features.name: Momentum
features.description: Quand le Démon réussit une attaque contre un PJ, vous gagnez une Peur.
features.trigger: Quand le démon réussit une attaque contre un pj, vous gagnez une peur

name: Demon of Hubris
description: Un démon d’une beauté parfaite et d’une cruauté infinie, armé d’une lance étincelante et vêtu de robes élégantes.

features.name: Terrifying
features.description: Quand le Démon réussit une attaque, tous les PJ à Portée Loin doivent perdre un Espoir et vous gagnez une Peur.

features.name: Double or Nothing
features.description: Quand un PJ à Portée Loin rate un jet, il peut choisir de relancer son Dé de Peur et prendre le nouveau résultat. S’il échoue encore, il coche 2 Stress et le Démon retire un Stress.

features.name: Unparalleled Skill
features.description: Infligez les dégâts d’attaque standard du Démon à une cible à Portée Proche.

features.name: The Root of Villainy
features.description: Placez sous le projecteur deux autres Démons à Portée Loin.

features.name: You Pale in Comparison
features.description: Quand un PJ rate un jet à Portée Proche du Démon, il doit cocher un Stress.
features.trigger: Quand un pj rate un jet à portée proche du démon, il doit cocher un stress

name: Demon of Jealousy
description: Une créature capricieuse aux membres grêles et aux désirs insatiables.
motives: Prendre ce qui appartient aux autres

features.name: Unprotected Mind
features.description: L’attaque standard du Démon inflige des dégâts directs.

features.name: My Turn
features.description: Quand le Démon coche des PV suite à une attaque, dépensez un nombre de Peurs égal aux PV cochés par le Démon pour faire cocher le même nombre de PV à l’attaquant.
features.trigger: Quand le démon coche des pv suite à une attaque

features.name: Rivalry
features.description: Quand une créature à Portée Proche subit des dégâts d’un autre adversaire, ajoutez un d4 au jet de dégâts.
features.trigger: Quand une créature à portée proche subit des dégâts d’un autre adversaire

features.name: What's Yours Is Mine
features.description: Quand un PJ subit des dégâts Sévères à Portée Très Proche du Démon, forcez la cible à faire un jet de Réaction de Finesse. En cas d’échec, le Démon s’empare d’un objet ou consommable de son choix dans l’inventaire de la cible.
features.trigger: Quand un pj subit des dégâts sévères à portée très proche du démon

name: Demon of Wrath
description: Un démon imposant aux poings gros comme des rochers, animé par une rage sans fin.

features.name: Anger Unrelenting
features.description: Les attaques du Démon infligent des dégâts directs.

features.name: Battle Lust
features.description: Faites bouillir le sang de tous les PJ à Portée Loin. Ils utilisent un d20 comme Dé de Peur jusqu’à la fin de la scène.

features.name: Retaliation
features.description: Quand le Démon subit des dégâts d’une attaque à Portée Proche, faites une attaque standard contre l’attaquant.
features.trigger: Quand le démon subit des dégâts d’une attaque à portée proche

features.name: Blood and Souls
features.description: Compte à rebours (Boucle 6). Activez la première fois qu’une attaque est effectuée à vue du Démon. Il décompte quand un PJ effectue une action violente. Quand il se déclenche, invoquez 1d4 Démons Mineurs, qui apparaissent à Portée Proche.
features.trigger: Quand un pj effectue une action violente

name: Devil Dealmaker
description: An imposing humanoid demon known for striking binding deals with punishments for failure.

features.name: Blood Pact
features.description: PCs can make binding deals with the Devil. When the PC succeeds or fails in a task related to their agreement the Devil appears during their next long rest to provide the reward or punishment.

features.name: Feeling the Heat
features.description: A Circle of illusory fire appears at Close range of the Devil. All PCs within the circle lose a Hope.

features.name: Time for Action
features.description: Spend a Fear to summon two Tier 3 Demons within Far range. Devil Planeswalker Devil Punisher Devils that had the ability to travel to different planes as an angel can escape the Circles Below, causing chaos elsewhere due to their corrupted nature.

name: Devil Planeswalker
description: A realm-traveling humanoid demon with horns and a devilish smirk.

features.name: Body Double
features.description: Mark a Stress to become Hidden and create a magical body double. The Devil can speak and act through the body double. The body double has the Devil's Difficulty and is reduced to ash if it moves beyond Very Far of the Devil or takes any damage.

features.name: Create Portal
features.description: Mark 2 Stress to open a portal within Close range to a known location in any realm. Once per scene, you can use this action to summon a Tier 3 Demon.

features.name: Banish
features.description: Spend a Fear to force a target within Far range to make an Instinct Reaction Roll. On a failure, the target is temporarily Banished to a different realm. When a PC rolls with Hope, the Banished target can make a new Instinct Reaction Roll with a -1 penalty applied to the Difficulty, ending the banishment on a success.

features.name: Blood Money
features.description: When a PC acts, you can offer a number of handfuls of gold if the PC attacks an ally instead of the Devil. If they accept, they gain the gold and have disadvantage on attacks against the Devil while it is in their possession. Punishers subdue new souls of the damned arriving into the Circles Below. Only when souls are sufficiently broken of spirit are they released for ownership by Devils and other demons as slaves.
features.trigger: When a PC acts

name: Devil Punisher
description: A brutish humanoid demon with horns and a love of violence.

features.name: Fire Lash
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Close range. On a success, deal 2dlO+l magic damage and pull the target into Melee range.

features.name: Circle of Fire
features.description: Mark a Stress to create a circle of magical fire around the Devil at Close range that lasts until the Devil takes Severe damage. Non-demon creatures who move through the fire must mark a HP.

features.name: Bind
features.description: Spend a Fear to create magical chains that wrap around a target. The target must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll or become Restrained and Vulnerable. While Restrained in this way, the target can't take actions except attempt to free themselves.

features.name: Punishment
features.description: When the Devil takes Major or greater damage from a PC's attack, you can mark a Stress to move a random card from the PC's loadout into their vault until the end of the scene.
features.trigger: When the Devil takes Major or greater damage from a PC's attack

features.name: No Hope
features.description: When the Devil makes a successful attack against a PC, they lose a Hope. Dinosaurs Dinosaurs are fearsome beasts from a distant era, one of the first attempts by the Forgotten Gods at creating life. Thought to have become extinct, the presence of dinosaurs indicates unnatural circumstances have occurred. Ankylosaurus An Ankylosaurus is a herbivore with thick armor and a clubbed tail. Quetzalcoatlus A Quetzalcoatlus is a flying reptile with a 30-foot wingspan. Triceratops A Triceratops is a herbivore with three horns and frilled neck.
features.trigger: When the Devil makes a successful attack against a PC

name: Dire Bat
description: Une chauve-souris aux larges ailes, éternellement loyale à son maître vampire.

features.name: Flying
features.description: En vol, la Chauve-souris gagne un bonus de +3 à sa Difficulté.

features.name: Screech
features.description: Envoyez un cri strident vers toutes les cibles devant la Chauve-souris à Portée Loin. Ces cibles doivent cocher 1d4 Stress.

features.name: Guardian
features.description: Quand un Vampire allié coche des PV, volez à Portée de Mêlée de l’attaquant et faites une attaque avec avantage contre lui. En cas de réussite, infligez 2d6+2 dégâts physiques.
features.trigger: Quand un vampire allié coche des pv

name: Drake
description: A small flying draconic beast.

features.name: Flying
features.description: While flying, the Drake gains a +2 bonus to their Difficulty. Minion (9): The Drake is defeated when they take any damage. For every 9 damage a PC deals to the Drake, defeat an additional Minion within range this attack would succeed against.

features.name: Group Attack
features.description: Spend a Fear to choose a target and spotlight all Drakes within Close range of them. Those Minions move into Very Close range of the target and make one shared attack roll. On a success, they deal 8 magic damage each. Combine this damage. Seadragon The Seadragon is a larger and more aggressive cousin of the seahorse. Seadragons can be trained as underwater mounts. Wyrmling Wyrmlings are dragon hatchlings. They have unnatural size and intelligence for creatures so young, and can quickly learn to control their breath weapon and fly.

name: Drakona Monk
description: A drakona skilled in martial arts.

features.name: Scales
features.description: You can mark a Stress to mark 1 fewer HP when the Drakona takes Severe damage.

features.name: Flurry of Blows
features.description: Mark a Stress and make attack rolls against a target within Melee range until you fail. For each success, deal ldl2 physical damage. Combine the damage.

features.name: Block Chi
features.description: When the target of the Drakona's attack marks HP, move a random card from the PC's loadout into their vault until the end of the scene. Dwarves Dwarves are short and stocky humanoids. Dwarves have an innate and magical connection to the earth. Dwarven settlements are found deep within the hills and mountains, with an economy typically centered on mining metals and gemstones. Dwarves support their underground settlements by clearing and farming the surrounding lands. This frequently brings Dwarves into conflict with woodland folk such as elves. Dwarf Cleric Clerics worship the God of the forge, granting power over the earth. They make excellent prospectors for their ability to sense metals. Dwarf Ironclad Ironclads are warriors clad head to toe in metal, making them virtually indestructible. Maddened Dwarf Maddened Dwarves were miners who lost their minds from isolation in the tunnels deep beneath the earth.
features.trigger: When the target of the Drakona's attack marks HP

name: Dryad
description: Un esprit de la nature sous la forme d’un arbre humanoïde.

features.name: Bramble Patch
features.description: Ciblez un point à Portée Loin. Créez un parterre d’épines couvrant une zone à Portée Proche. Toutes les cibles dans cette zone subissent 2d6+2 dégâts physiques quand elles agissent. Une cible doit réussir un jet de Finesse ou infliger plus de 20 dégâts à la Dryade pour quitter la zone.

features.name: Grow Saplings
features.description: Faites pousser trois Minions Arbrisseau Tréant, qui apparaissent à Portée Proche et passent immédiatement sous le projecteur.

features.name: We Are All One
features.description: Quand un allié meurt à Portée Proche, retirez 2 PV et 2 Stress car la force vitale de l’allié déchu retourne à la forêt.
features.trigger: Quand un allié meurt à portée proche

name: Earth Giant
description: A giant capable of moving and shaking the earth.

features.name: Stoneskin
features.description: The Giant is resistant to physical damage.

features.name: Crushing Blow
features.description: When a target uses armor to reduce damage from the Giant's standard attack, they must mark 2 Armor Slots.

features.name: Darkness
features.description: Mark a Stress to extinguish all sources of non-magical light up to Far range. Targets in darkness who can't see lose a Hope and have disadvantage on action rolls.

features.name: Quake
features.description: Mark a Stress to make the ground shake. All targets within Far range must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll or fall down, becoming Vulnerable until they next act.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Giant makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.
features.trigger: When the Giant makes a successful attack against a PC

name: Ekoko
description: A large winged beast that relies on echolocation.

features.name: Echolocation
features.description: The Ekoko is blind but has an acute sense of hearing. PCs can't attempt to become Hidden based on line of sight. Instead, a PC can become Hidden by succeeding on a Finesse Roll (15). Relentless (3): The Ekoko can be spotlighted up to three times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Screech
features.description: Mark a Stress to emit a deafening screech and learn the location of all creatures within Far range. All targets within Close range must mark 1d4 Stress.

features.name: Dive-Bomb
features.description: Mark a Stress and choose a point within Close range. The Ekoko leaps and glides to that point. Make an attack against all creatures within Very Close range. Targets the Ekoko succeeds against take 2d8+2 physical damage and must lose a Hope.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Ekoko makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear. Elder Fae The Elder Fae are powerful beings of the Fae Realm. Elder Fae tend to have eccentric hobbies and interests, varying from provoking nobles at Fae court, to thrill seeking, to collecting odd items such as people’s names, oaths, or memories. Elder Fae Noble Nobles attend the Fae courts and most often have the appearance of Elves, Faeries, or Fauns, often with exaggerated features from their time in the Fae Realm. Elder Fae Recluse Recluses keep to their own domains in the Fae Realm. They have a large, monstrous appearance from living apart from civilization for so long, building up a hoard of items in their collection. Elder Fae Wanderer Wanderers travel the Fey Realm and beyond in search of new experiences and adventure. They are able to sense when their name is spoken by others, and will often investigate. The Fae doesn’t harm others, instead they get their foes to attack themselves with a snap of their fingers.
features.trigger: When the Ekoko makes a successful attack against a PC

name: Elemental Spark
description: Un éclat flamboyant de feu élémentaire.

features.name: Minion (9)
features.description: L’Élémentaire est vaincu quand il subit n’importe quels dégâts. Pour chaque tranche de 9 dégâts qu’un PJ inflige à l’Élémentaire, vainquez un Minion supplémentaire à portée que l’attaque toucherait.

features.name: Group Attack
features.description: Choisissez une cible et placez sous le projecteur toutes les Étincelles Élémentaires à Portée Proche d’elle. Ces Minions se déplacent à Portée de Mêlée de la cible et font un jet d’attaque partagé. En cas de réussite, ils infligent 5 dégâts physiques chacun. Combinez ces dégâts.

name: Elf Spellblade
description: An elf who combines skill with magic and swordplay.

features.name: Shimmershield
features.description: Mark a Stress to create a shimmering shield around the Elf. When an opponent succeeds on an attack roll, they must roll a d4. On a roll of 1 or 2 they hit the shield instead of the Elf, and the shield disappears. On a roll of 3 or 4 they deal damage to the Elf as normal.

features.name: Teleport
features.description: Mark a Stress to teleport to a position up to Far range.

features.name: Kinetic Blast
features.description: Mark a Stress to push a target up to Close range. If the target is knocked into an object, deal 1d6 physical damage. Combine this damage with attack damage if used as an infused strike.

features.name: Infused Strikes
features.description: When the Elf succeeds on a standard attack, you can spend a Fear to also use either the Teleport or Kinetic Blast action.
features.trigger: When the Elf succeeds on a standard attack

name: Eyeball Aberration
description: A monstrous spherical arcane beast with one huge eye, mouths on stalks, and a long, lolling tongue.

features.name: Hovering
features.description: The Aberration hovers several feet above the ground and has advantage on Reaction Rolls to avoid being moved orRestrained.

features.name: Relentless (2)
features.description: The Aberration can be spotlighted 2 times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Crushing Regard
features.description: Mark a Stressand choose a target within Close range. The target must make a Strength Reaction roll or take3d20phy damage and becomeVulnerableuntil their next roll with Hope.

features.name: Unmaking
features.description: Spend 2 Fearto pick a point within Far range as the Aberration shreds the fabric of reality in the area. Every creature within Close range of the point must make a Presence Reaction Roll or take3d12direct magic damage. Mundane terrain, buildings, and objects within Close range of the point are severely damaged or destroyed.

features.name: Biting Stalks
features.description: When the Aberration takes damage from a target within Melee range,mark a Stressto make an attack against that target. On a success, the target takes3d8+4phy damage.
features.trigger: When the Aberration takes damage from a target within Melee range

name: Faerie Godmother
description: A faerie with thick-rimmed glasses and a magic wand.

features.name: Grow Wings
features.description: Mark a Stress to grow wings on 1d4 allies, granting them the ability to fly. The wings last until after the target makes 3 action rolls or until their next rest.

features.name: Bewitch
features.description: Spend a Fear to force a target to make an Instinct Reaction Roll. On a failure, they begin to turn into a different ancestry, starting a Transformation Countdown (4). The countdown ticks down each GM turn. When triggered, the target instantly transforms and the new ancestry replaces their existing ancestry. If the Faerie is defeated, all transformation countdowns end.

features.name: Luckbender
features.description: Once per scene, when an ally within Far range misses, you can mark a Stress to reroll the attack.

name: Faerie Spider
description: A large spicier that catches Faerie and can become invisible.

features.name: Web Walker
features.description: The Spider can move freely along webbing.

features.name: Invisibility
features.description: Mark a Stress to become Invisible until after the Spider's next attack. While Invisible, the Spider has advantage on attack rolls and attack rolls made against them have disadvantage.

features.name: Cocoon
features.description: Make an attack against a target Restrained by webbing within Melee range. On a success, they lose a Hope as the Spider wraps them in a cocoon of webbing. The target must free themselves with a Strength or Finesse Roll before they can take other actions.

features.name: Webbing
features.description: When another creature moves to Close range or further, you can spend a Fear to reveal invisible webbing set anywhere on the battlefield by the Spider. The target must make a Strength Reaction Roll. On a failure, they become Restrained and Vulnerable until they break free with a successful Strength Roll (13).
features.trigger: When another creature moves to Close range or further

name: Faerie Wrathwing
description: A faerie mage thralled by the queen’s power and influence.

features.name: Flight
features.description: While flying, the Wrathwing gains a +2 bonus to their Difficulty.

features.name: Exhausting Curse
features.description: Spend a Fearto make an attack against a target within Far range. On a success, the target takes3d6magic damage and, for the rest of the scene, when the target spends Hope they must also mark a Stress.

features.name: Gust of Wind
features.description: Mark a Stressto create a buffeting wind in front of the Wrathwing within Close range. All targets in the area must succeed on a Strength Saving Throw or be knocked back to Far range.

features.name: Hail of Jagged Stone
features.description: Mark a Stressand choose a point within Far range. Stalactites drop in a Very Close area around that point. All targets within that area must make an Agility Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take2d10 physical damage and areRestraineduntil they break free with a Strength Roll (10). Targets who succeed take half damage and are notRestrained.

features.name: Luckbender
features.description: If the Wrathwing is targeted by an attack,mark a Stressand force the attacker to roll with disadvantage.
features.trigger: If the Wrathwing is targeted by an attack

name: False God
description: A cult leader who is empowered through worship of them.

features.name: Kneel Before Me
features.description: Mark a Stress to force all targets within Far range to make a Presence Reaction Roll. Targets who fail are forced to their knees, becoming Vulnerable until they next act.

features.name: Rise, My Warrior
features.description: Spend a Fear to grant a worshipper a permanent +2 bonus on attack rolls and an additional damage die when rolling damage.

features.name: Seek Glory, My Followers
features.description: Once per scene, spend a Fear to spotlight all other adversaries within Far range. Attacks they make while spotlighted in this way deal half damage.

features.name: Smite the Heretics
features.description: When the False God succeeds on an attack, you can mark a Stress to gain a +10 bonus to the damage roll. Golems Golems are constructs created to complete simple manual labor tasks, whether as a cheaper option to hiring workers or due to the hazards or difficulties involved in undertaking the task. Golems have runic operating instructions carved into their exterior. During combat the runes degrade as the Golem takes damage, with unpredictable outcomes. Clay Golem Clay Golems are the simplest type of Golem, assembled and hand sculpted from clay. They are used for menial tasks such as hauling mine carts and pulling carriages.
features.trigger: When the False God succeeds on an attack

name: Fatespinner Hag
description: A tall, stooped crone with elongated arms, who can pull and influence invisible threads of fate.

features.name: Bias Fate
features.description: You gain 2 Fear when a PC rolls with Fear.

features.name: Coven
features.description: Increase the Hag's Difficulty by 1 for each Hag within Far range.

features.name: Read Fate
features.description: Mark a Stress to gain advantage on an attack as the Hag reads the target's future.

features.name: Entangled Fates
features.description: Mark a Stress to entangle the fates of two targets within Very Close range of each other until the end of the scene. Both targets must make an Instinct Reaction Roll. On two failures, when one target takes damage, the other also takes the same damage. On one failure, when one target takes damage the other target must mark a Stress.

features.name: Coven Leader
features.description: Once per scene, spend a Fear to spotlight all Hags within Far range. Attacks they make while spotlighted in this way deal half damage.

features.name: Fighting Fate
features.description: When a PC changes the outcome of a die roll, they must mark a Stress. Mutater Hag Mystic Hag Mutater Hags have the ability to change in people or the surrounding environment. They can alter an individual’s appearance or memories, and can warp the surrounding environment. Mystic Hags have the ability to affect the senses of people around them. They can create illusory duplicates of themselves, which are real enough to form a coven with themselves.
features.trigger: When a PC changes the outcome of a die roll

name: Fear Scourged
description: A person overcome with fear, surrounded by fear spirits in the form of floating spectral skulls.

features.name: Cloaking Spirits
features.description: Increase the Scourged's Difficulty against attacks by the number of unmarked Stress.

features.name: Counter Emotion
features.description: When a PC spends Hope, mark a Stress.

features.name: All Hope is Lost
features.description: All targets within Close range lose 1 Hope. If they have no Hope, you gain a Fear.

features.name: Fear Barrage
features.description: Mark a Stress to make an attack against a target within Close range. On a success, the target takes 3d10+3 magic damage and they use a d20 as their Fear Die until the end of the scene.

features.name: Fear Is Fuel
features.description: When a PC within Close range rolls a failure with Fear, clear a Stress.
features.trigger: When a PC within Close range rolls a failure with Fear

name: Fellmounted Shadow King
description: The shade of a forgotten ruler, cursed to patrol the skies atop a winged eel in the service of an evil demiurge.

features.name: Light and Shadow
features.description: The Shadow King gains a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls while in shadow or darkness and a -1 penalty to attack and damage rolls while in bright light.

features.name: Relic Hunter
features.description: The Shadow King has advantage on attacks against PCs with magical weapons or armor equipped.

features.name: Hellsong
features.description: Spend 2 Fear to have the Shadow King's mount unleash a spine-chilling screech that echoes over the battlefield. All PCs within Far range must make a Presence Reaction Roll (18). Targets who succeed must mark a Stress. Targets who fail must mark a Stress and lose a Hope. If the PC can't lose a Hope, you gain 2 Fear.

features.name: Air Support
features.description: Three times per scene, you can spend a Fear to have an adversary within Far range of the Shadow King reroll a missed attack with advantage.
features.trigger: When an adversary within Far range misses an attack

name: Feral Vampire
description: A cursed humanoid with a frothing mouth behind their sharp teeth.

features.name: Sunlight Vulnerability
features.description: The Vampire is Vulnerable when in direct sunlight.

features.name: Blood Frenzy
features.description: The Vampire has advantage on attack rolls against targets who have marked HP in the scene.

features.name: Smell Fear
features.description: The Vampire can detect a Hidden PC who has rolled with Fear during the scene.

features.name: Vampiric Bite
features.description: Mark a Stress and make a standard attack against a target within Melee range. On a success, deal 2d4+10 physical damage and the Vampire clears a HP. If the target marks HP, you can spend a Fear to start a Vampirism Countdown (3). The countdown ticks down after each long rest. When it triggers, the target is inflicted with a Curse of Vampirism. Once per long rest, when the cursed creature makes an attack against a target in Melee range, they can mark a Stress to bite the target, adding 2d4 physical damage to the total damage dealt and clearing a HP. The cursed creature is also Vulnerable while in direct sunlight. Vampire Mage Vampires who are also spellcasters can heighten the effects of the curse of vampirism on themselves - both good and bad. They gain new powers but have weaknesses that can be exploited.

name: Ferryman
description: A cloaked skeletal guide to newly formed souls.

features.name: Darkness
features.description: Mark a Stress to create a cloud of magical darkness up to Close range which can be dispelled by bright light. Targets in darkness who can't see lose a Hope and have disadvantage on action rolls.

features.name: Shadow Tethers
features.description: Spend a Fear and choose a point within Far range. All targets within Very Close range of that point must succeed on a Strength Reaction Roll or become Restrained until they break free with a Strength Roll.

features.name: Void Cloak
features.description: When a ranged attack misses the Ferryman, you can mark a Stress to redirect it back at the attacker, dealing half damage. Reaper Reapers are dispatched to claim mortal souls that are trapped or otherwise overdue to cross the Veil. They also perform judgment on souls, determining whether they belong in the Hallows Above or Circles Below. Some Reapers are renegades, unsatisfied with their duties, and attempt to end the cycle of life by working to bring about an apocalyptic event.
features.trigger: When a ranged attack misses the Ferryman

name: Fire Beetles
description: A swarm of fist-sized red beetles which feed on burning coals and hibernate in the ground or trees until awakened by an active blaze.

features.name: Horde (3d4+2)
features.description: When the Fire Beetles have marked half or more of their HP, their standard attack deals3d4+2physical damage instead.

features.name: Swarm
features.description: Spend a Fearto swarm a target within Close range. The Beetles cover the body of the target, biting and sparking. Make a standard attack, on a success the target must make a Presence Reaction Roll. On a failure, the target loses 1 Hope and becomesVulnerable until their next roll with Hope. On a success, the target marks 1 Stress.

name: Fire Giant
description: A giant wielding precise control over fire.

features.name: Burn
features.description: Mark a Stress to heat a metal object. Targets holding the object can choose to drop it. Otherwise, the target takes 2d12+5 direct magic damage.

features.name: Fireball
features.description: Spend a Fear and choose a point within Far range. The area within Very Close range of that point explodes with energy. All targets within the area must make an Agility Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take 2d20 magic damage. Targets who succeed take half damage.

features.name: Fire Shield
features.description: When an attack succeeds against the Giant, you can mark a Stress to form a shield of fire. Reduce the damage by 1d8 and deal that damage to all creatures within Melee range.
features.trigger: When an attack succeeds against the Giant

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Giant makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear. Frost Giant Storm Giant Frost Giants live in extreme cold environments such as mountain peaks and polar regions. Sea Giant Sea Giants live in underwater homes built from coral or shipwrecks.
features.trigger: When the Giant makes a successful attack against a PC

name: Flame Fanner
description: A small, imp-like creature often seen in proximity to Ignocerouses.

features.name: Flight
features.description: While flying, the Flame Fanner has a +2 bonus to their Difficulty.

features.name: Fan the Flames
features.description: Spend a Fearto furiously beat the Fanner’s wings and reignite a suppressed Blazing Trail made by an Ignocerous in Very Close range. When this Action is used, an Ignocerous within Close range is healed for 1 HP.

features.name: Quick Reflexes
features.description: When the Flame Fanner takes damage from an attack within Close range, you canmark a Stressto take half damage.
features.trigger: When the Flame Fanner takes damage from an attack within Close range

name: Fossil
description: The animated skeleton of a formerly great beast.

features.name: Pack Tactics
features.description: If the Fossil makes a successful standard attack and another Fossil is within Melee range of the target, deal 3d8+7 physical damage instead of their standard damage.

features.name: Bone Shards
features.description: When the Fossil takes Severe damage, all creatures within Melee range take 2dlO physical damage. Skeletal Monstrosity Monstrosities have humanoid appearance and are formed from the assembly of an assortment of bones. A large cavity in the chest is used by the Monstrosity as a cage.
features.trigger: When the Fossil takes Severe damage

name: Frost Bear
description: A large polar bear surrounded by a cloud of frost.

features.name: Ice Trail
features.description: The Bear leaves a trail of frost behind them when they move. Creatures who move through the frost must mark a Stress.

features.name: Chilling Breath
features.description: Mark a Stress and make an attack against all foes in a line up to Close range. On a success, deal 2d12 magic damage. Targets that take Major or greater damage are Restrained by ice until they break free with a successful Strength Roll (15).

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Bear makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.
features.trigger: When the Bear makes a successful attack against a PC

features.name: Flash Freeze
features.description: When a creature makes a successful attack against the Bear from within Melee range, they must mark a Stress and can't move when they next act. Lightning Crab Lightning Crabs are found near the shore in areas affected by magical storms. They move speedily and channel their inner energy into delivering electric shocks. The annualmigration ofthe lightning crabs was a briefbut I spectacular sight. - Archibald
features.trigger: When a creature makes a successful attack against the Bear from within Melee range

name: Frost Giant
description: A giant who thrives in frigid environments.

features.name: Freeze/Thaw
features.description: Mark a Stress to freeze or thaw the surface of a body of water up to Far range.

features.name: Icicles
features.description: Spend a Fear and make an attack against up to three targets within Close range, flinging a barrage of icicles. Targets the Giant succeeds against take 4d6+2 physical damage.

features.name: Frost Shield
features.description: When an attack succeeds against the Giant, you can mark a Stress to form a shield of ice. Reduce the damage by 1d8 and deal that damage to all creatures within Melee range.
features.trigger: When an attack succeeds against the Giant

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Giant makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear. Storm Giants can be found where the weather is unpredictable. They can shape weather patterns to bring fierce storms.
features.trigger: When the Giant makes a successful attack against a PC

name: Frost Spirits
description: A collection of animated motes of cold energy.

features.name: Arcane Form
features.description: The Spirits are resistant to magic damage. Horde (2d6+l): When the Spirits have marked half or more of their HP, their standard attack deals 2d6+l magic damage instead.

features.name: Freeze
features.description: When the Spirits deal Major or greater damage, the target can't move when they next act. Minor Nature Spirit Nature Spirits are created from the will of nature. Up close they take the form of translucent animal beings like rabbits or foxes. Wisp A Wisp (or Will-o’-Wisp) is the Spirit of a mortal, which takes the form of a mote of light. Depending on the mortal’s former nature the Spirit can be helpful or harmful.
features.trigger: When the Spirits deal Major or greater damage

name: Galapa Chronomancer
description: A galapa wielding a wooden staff with a glowing crystal at the tip.

features.name: Timebending
features.description: The Galapa can choose the target of their standard attack after making their attack roll.

features.name: Slow Time
features.description: Spend a Fear to slow time progression for all targets within Very Close range. Targets must make an Instinct Reaction Roll. On a failure, when the target is spotlighted and they don't have a token, they can't act yet. They gain a token and describe what they're preparing to do, with the spotlight passing to the GM. When they are spotlighted and they have a token, they clear the token and can act. This effect lasts until the Galapa takes Severe damage, takes a new action, or a target has a token and marks 2 Stress.

features.name: Retract
features.description: When the Galapa takes physical damage, you can mark a Stress to take half damage.
features.trigger: When the Galapa takes physical damage

name: Genie
description: A humanoid with a gaseous body, dressed in fine silks and jewelry. Motives & Tactics: Create home, defend master, grant wishes

features.name: Magical Being
features.description: The Genie starts with up to 3 Wish tokens and a magical home, an extradimensional space housed within a mundane item. The Genie becomes bound to any creature taking possession of the item housing the Genie's home, and must lend aid to them. If all Wish tokens are expended, you can mark 3 HP to create a new home and gain 3 Wish tokens.

features.name: Weapon Trick
features.description: Mark a Stress to magically transform the weapons of all PCs within Close range into mundane items. The next successful attack made with the item deals half damage and triggers a wild magic effect (Introduction of this book), after which it reverts back to normal.

features.name: Grant Wish
features.description: Spend a Wish token to grant a request to someone that the Genie is bound to. The Genie can alter a target's form or create a portal to help fulfil the request.

features.name: Curse
features.description: When a PC offends the Genie, you can spend a Fear to force them to make an Instinct Reaction Roll. On a failure, the target temporarily turns into an animal or object until they mark 2 Stress to revert.
features.trigger: When a PC offends the Genie

features.name: Reform
features.description: When the Genie marks their last HP, spend a Wish token to reform over a long rest in the Genie's magical home. It turns out the Geniegets the last laugh whenyou askfor infinite wishes… -	Archibald Ghosts Ghosts are souls that remain in the Mortal Realm after death, appearing as spectral entities in the form of their former self. They have refused to cross the Veil until a last unresolved matter is corrected. Ghost A Ghost might linger due to unconfessed love, bringing their murderer to justice, or to watch their plans come to fruition. Ghosts can try to possess mortals to carry out this one last task. Ghostly Legion The sudden death of large numbers of people can create a Ghostly Legion. A Ghostly Legion also forms when souls have been trapped and collected, with the souls still possessing enough spirit to fight.
features.trigger: When the Genie marks their last HP

name: Ghostly Legion
description: A restless group of spirited souls driven by one final collective desire.

features.name: Incorporeal Form
features.description: The Legion is resistant to physical damage. Mark a Stress to move up to Close range through solid objects.

features.name: Possess
features.description: Spend a Fear and mark a HP to attempt to take control of all PCs within Very Close range. Targets must succeed on an Instinct Reaction Roll or become Possessed by a soul from the Legion until they succeed on an Instinct Roll to end the possession. Attacks made against the Possessed target damage the target, after which they can attempt a new Instinct Reaction Roll to end the possession. You can spotlight a Possessed target. Roll a d20 instead of Duality Dice to make an attack, and if you use an ability that costs Hope, spend Fear instead.

features.name: Overwhelm
features.description: When the Legion takes Minor damage from an attack within Melee range, you can mark a Stress to make a standard attack with advantage against the attacker. Giants Giants are large humanoids with one to three eyes through which they sense patterns in nature. By tracing these patterns, they can capture energy as runes that they carry with them. Giants can then retrace the pattern to release the rune’s power. Cloud Giant Cloud Giants live in castles atop solid clouds in the sky. While they use air runes to fly, some have tamed large, winged creatures to use as a mount. Earth Giant Earth Giants live underground where they shape the earth to their liking. In the darkness they can enter long-term meditative trances, appearing like statues, and are hostile if their peace is disturbed. Fire Giant Fire Giants prefer high temperature environments, so tend to be found in extreme locations such as deserts or volcano lairs.
features.trigger: When the Legion takes Minor damage from an attack within Melee range

name: Giant Tortoise
description: A huge tortoise as tall as a building.

features.name: Slow
features.description: When you spotlight the Tortoise and they don't have a token on their stat block, they can't act yet. Place a token on their stat block and describe what they're preparing to do. When you spotlight the Tortoise and they have a token on their stat block, clear the token.

features.name: Ground Slam
features.description: All targets within Very Close range must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll or fall down, becoming Vulnerable until they next act.

features.name: Shell Retreat
features.description: When an attack succeeds against the Tortoise, you can mark a Stress to retreat into the shell. The Tortoise halves incoming damage until they emerge.
