While in the shell the Tortoise can't move and they make attack rolls with disadvantage. Gorillas are large apes that often coexist with the more-intelligent Simiah. Narwhal Narwhals are whales with a long horn protruding from their head and are often used as a mount by undersea creatures.
features.trigger: When an attack succeeds against the Tortoise

name: Gnome Alchemist
description: A gnome wearing a face mask who specializes in creating chemical concoctions.

features.name: Built Up Tolerance
features.description: The Gnome is resistant to magic damage.

features.name: Transmute
features.description: When the Gnome acts, you can mark a Stress to roll a d6. On a 5 or 6, a handful of a gold belonging to a PC is turned to lead.

features.name: Poison Cloud
features.description: Spend a Fear to don a mask and release a vial of poisonous gas which spreads out to Far range and lasts until the end of the scene. Targets within this area take 2d10+l direct physical damage when they act and have disadvantage on action rolls.

features.name: Acidic Puddles
features.description: The Gnome's standard attacks leave acidic puddles and all creatures who move through it take 3d6 physical damage.

name: Gorge Buzzard
description: An enormous carrion raptor with stark plumage and a voracious appetite.

features.name: Broken Bones
features.description: The Gorge Buzzard’s standard attack deals direct damage.

features.name: Boulder Dash
features.description: Spend a Fearto grab a heavy rock to lob at a group within Very Far range. All targets within Very Close range of the landing point must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll. Targets that fail take3d10+15 physical damage and areVulnerableuntil they mark a Stress, which they can do at any time.

features.name: Acid Vomit
features.description: When the Buzzard marks HP from physical damage within Close range,spend a Fearto make an attack against all targets in a line. This line must include the attacker. Targets the Buzzard succeeds against take3d8+10physical damage and must mark 2 Armor Slots without receiving their benefits (they can still use armor to reduce the damage). If they can’t mark an Armor Slot, they must mark an additional HP and you gain a Fear. Then move the Buzzard to a point within Far range.
features.trigger: When the Buzzard marks HP from physical damage within Close range

features.name: Corpse Haul
features.description: When a target within Very Close range fails an action roll against the Buzzard, they must succeed on a Strength Reaction Roll. If they rolled a failure with Hope, they have advantage on this roll. On a failure, the target isRestrainedin the Buzzard’s Claws until they succeed on a Strength or Finesse Roll.
features.trigger: When a target within Very Close range fails an action roll against the Buzzard

features.name: Reflective Feathers
features.description: When the Buzzard is targeted by a spellcast roll,mark a Stressto target a PC within Far range. If the spellcast roll is equal to or higher than their evasion, they are affected by the spell as well.
features.trigger: When the Buzzard is targeted by a spellcast roll

name: Greater Earth Elemental
description: Un glissement de terrain vivant fait de rochers et de poussière, grand comme une maison.

features.name: Slow
features.description: Quand vous placez l’Élémentaire sous le projecteur et qu’il n’a pas de jeton sur sa fiche, il ne peut pas encore agir. Placez un jeton. Quand il est mis sous le projecteur avec un jeton, retirez-le et il peut agir.

features.name: Crushing Blows
features.description: Quand l’Élémentaire réussit une attaque, la cible doit cocher un Emplacement d’armure sans en recevoir les bénéfices. Si elle ne peut pas, elle doit cocher un PV supplémentaire.

features.name: Immovable Object
features.description: Une attaque qui déplacerait l’Élémentaire le déplace de deux portées de moins. Quand l’Élémentaire subit des dégâts physiques, réduisez-les de 7.

features.name: Rockslide
features.description: Créez un éboulement devant l’Élémentaire à Portée Proche. Toutes les cibles doivent faire un jet de Réaction d’Agilité (19). Les cibles qui échouent subissent 2d12+5 dégâts physiques et deviennent Vulnérables jusqu’à leur prochain jet avec Espoir. Les cibles qui réussissent subissent la moitié des dégâts.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: Quand l’Élémentaire réussit une attaque contre un PJ, vous gagnez une Peur.
features.trigger: Quand l’élémentaire réussit une attaque contre un pj, vous gagnez une peur

name: Greater Water Elemental
description: Une immense vague vivante qui s’abat sur les ennemis.

features.name: Water Jet
features.description: Attaquez une cible à Portée Très Proche. En cas de réussite, infligez 2d4+7 dégâts physiques et la prochaine action de la cible a un désavantage. En cas d’échec, la cible doit cocher un Stress.

features.name: Drowning Embrace
features.description: Attaquez toutes les cibles à Portée Très Proche. Les cibles touchées deviennent Entravées et Vulnérables car elles commencent à se noyer. Une cible peut se libérer avec un jet de Force ou d’Instinct réussi.

features.name: High Tide
features.description: Quand l’Élémentaire réussit une attaque standard, repoussez la cible à Portée Proche.
features.trigger: Quand l’élémentaire réussit une attaque standard, repoussez la cible à portée proche

name: Gulch Salamander: Burning Lizard
description: A long, multi-legged lizard with fire that burns from its back during the day and smoulders at night.

features.name: Flame Absorption
features.description: The Burning Lizard is immune to damage from fire or heat and instead clears a HP when subjected to it.

features.name: Burning Breath
features.description: Spend a Fearto breathe a gout of flames in front of the Burning Lizard at Very Close range. All targets in this area must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take2d6+6physical damage and areBurntuntil they mark a Stress to remove the condition. If they do so, you gain a Fear. While Burnt, a target has disadvantage on Agility, Finesse and Strength Rolls.

features.name: Day’s End (Phase Change)
features.description: If the sun is down, or the Gulch Salamander is damaged by something cold, replace them with Sluggish Lizard.
features.trigger: If the sun is down

name: Gulch Salamander: Sluggish Lizard
description: A long, multi-legged lizard with fire that burns from its back during the day and smoulders at night.

features.name: Minion (7)
features.description: The Sluggish Lizard is defeated when they take any damage (see “Ignition”). For every 7 damage a PC deals to the Sluggish Lizard, defeat an additional Minion within range the attack would succeed against.

features.name: Group Attack
features.description: Spend a Fearto choose a target and spotlight all Sluggish Lizards within Close range of them. Those Minions move into Melee range of the target and make one shared attack roll. On a success, they deal 7 physical damage each. Combine this damage.

features.name: Ignition (Phase Change)
features.description: If the Sluggish Lizard is subjected to damage from fire or heat, they instead take no damage. Replace them with a Burning Lizard.
features.trigger: If the Sluggish Lizard is subjected to damage from fire or heat

name: Halfling Underlord
description: A suave halfling criminal boss.

features.name: Lucky
features.description: The Halfling can reroll a 1 on an action roll.

features.name: Give Orders
features.description: Mark a Stress to spotlight 1d4+l allies. Attacks they make while spotlighted in this way deal half damage.

features.name: Silver Tongue
features.description: Spend a Fear to force a target within Close range to make a Presence Reaction Roll. On a failure, the target becomes Charmed. While Charmed, the target must make a Presence Reaction Roll when they act. On a success, they end the condition. On a failure, they act on the Halfling's orders.

features.name: Poisoned Blade
features.description: When a target of the Halfling's attack marks HP, they become Sluggish until they clear HP. While Sluggish, their Evasion is halved and Agility Rolls are made with disadvantage.
features.trigger: When a target of the Halfling's attack marks HP

name: Head Vampire
description: Un mort-vivant captivant vêtu de fines parures aristocratiques.

features.name: Terrifying
features.description: Quand le Vampire réussit une attaque, tous les PJ à Portée Loin perdent un Espoir et vous gagnez une Peur.

features.name: Look into My Eyes
features.description: Une créature qui se déplace à Portée de Mêlée du Vampire doit faire un jet de Réaction d’Instinct. En cas d’échec, vous gagnez 1d4 Peurs.

features.name: Feed on Followers
features.description: Quand le Vampire est à Portée de Mêlée d’un allié, faites cocher un PV à l’allié. Le Vampire retire alors un PV.

features.name: The Hunt Is On
features.description: Invoquez 1d4 Vampires, qui apparaissent à Portée Loin et passent immédiatement sous le projecteur.

features.name: Lifesuck
features.description: Quand le Vampire est mis sous le projecteur, lancez un d8. Sur un résultat de 6 ou plus, toutes les cibles à Portée Très Proche doivent cocher un PV.
features.trigger: Quand le vampire est mis sous le projecteur, lancez un d8

name: Herald of Conquest
description: A majestic messenger adorned in menacing finery, delivering news of imminent conquest to a nervous populace.

features.name: Aura of Superiority
features.description: Any time a PC rolls with Fear while in the presence of the Herald, they must mark a Stress.

features.name: Your Doom Comes
features.description: Spend 2 Fearto have the Herald deliver news of their master’s imminent arrival to conquer the local community, boasting about the Warlord’s many victories. All targets within Far range must make a Presence Reaction Roll or mark 2 Stress.

features.name: Silence, Worm!
features.description: Mark a Stressand force a target within Far range to make a Presence Reaction Roll. On a failure, they becomeCowed. WhileCowed, they areVulnerableand must mark a Stress any time they speak out against the Herald. The target clearsCowedon a success with Hope or during their next rest.

features.name: Withering Contempt
features.description: When a PC makes a Presence Roll against the Herald,mark a Stressto give the roll disadvantage.
features.trigger: When a PC makes a Presence Roll against the Herald

features.name: My Master Is Not Without Mercy
features.description: Any target that pledges loyalty or surrenders pro- actively becomesSuborned. ASubornedtarget cannot clear Stress but is granted a reprieve from the worst of the oncoming conquest. A target can clearSubornedby taking dangerous action against the Herald’s master. Eyeball aberration

name: Huge Golden Ooze
description: A large moving mound of golden slime with a treasure visible at the center.

features.name: Slow
features.description: When you spotlight the Ooze and they don't have a token on their stat block, they can't act yet. Place a token on their stat block and describe what they're preparing to do. When you spotlight the Ooze and they have a token on their stat block, clear the token and they can act.

features.name: Precious Loot
features.description: The Ooze has approximately 4 bags of gold (or other items worth the same value) within them. A PC can mark a HP to make a Finesse Roll (13) and pull 2d4 handfuls of gold from the Ooze.

features.name: Sense Gold
features.description: Mark a Stress to make a standard attack against a target carrying gold within range. On a success, deal damage and the target loses a handful of gold.

features.name: Consume Gold
features.description: Countdown (4). When the Ooze is in the spotlight for the first time, activate the countdown. It ticks down when a PC rolls with Fear against the Ooze. When it ticks down, one bag of gold is consumed. When it triggers, the last of the gold is consumed.
features.trigger: Countdown (4)

features.name: Absorb Gold
features.description: When the Ooze moves over 1 bag's worth of gold, tick up the countdown and absorb the gold.
features.trigger: When the Ooze moves over 1 bag's worth of gold

features.name: Split
features.description: When the Ooze has 4 or more HP marked, you can spend a Fear to split them into two Golden Oozes, or two Clear Oozes if the countdown has triggered. Immediately spotlight both of them. Orcs Orcs are rugged humanoids with tusks. They are fiercely stubborn and independent and will stand up against anyone they don’t respect, which can quickly escalate to violence. Many Orcs prefer to work for themselves over others. As such, Orcish societies can be rather anarchic, with self-employed workers who rarely collaborate and constantly undercut each other to stay ahead.
features.trigger: When the Ooze has 4 or more HP marked

name: Huge Green Ooze
description: Un monticule vert translucide d’acide plus grand que la plupart des humains.

features.name: Slow
features.description: Quand vous placez la Gelée sous le projecteur et qu’elle n’a pas de jeton sur sa fiche, elle ne peut pas encore agir. Placez un jeton. Quand elle est mise sous le projecteur avec un jeton, retirez-le et elle peut agir.

features.name: Acidic Form
features.description: Quand la Gelée réussit une attaque, la cible doit cocher un Emplacement d’armure sans en recevoir les bénéfices. Si elle ne peut pas, elle doit cocher un PV supplémentaire.

features.name: Envelop
features.description: Faites une attaque contre une cible à Portée de Mêlée. En cas de réussite, la Gelée l’Enveloppe et la cible doit cocher 2 Stress. Tant qu’Enveloppée, la cible doit cocher un Stress supplémentaire chaque fois qu’elle fait un jet d’action. Quand la Gelée subit des dégâts Sévères, toutes les cibles Enveloppées sont libérées.

features.name: Split
features.description: Quand la Gelée a 4 PV ou plus cochés, divisez-la en deux Gelées Vertes sans PV ni Stress cochés. Placez-les immédiatement toutes les deux sous le projecteur.
features.trigger: Quand la gelée a 4 pv ou plus cochés

name: Human Supervillain
description: A masked, cape-wearing flying villain out for revenge.

features.name: Deadly Flight
features.description: While flying, the Supervillain can move up to Far range instead of Close range before taking an action.

features.name: You Fell For My Trap
features.description: Mark a Stress to reveal a pit trap set anywhere on the battlefield by the Supervillain. All targets within Very Close range of the trap must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll (15) or fall into the trap until they escape with a Strength or Agility Roll (15). Start a Trap Countdown (4). When it triggers, the trap is filled with acid, lava, or spikes and all targets take 3d10 direct physical damage when they act.

features.name: Energy Blast
features.description: Spend a Fear and choose a point within Far range. All targets within Very Close range of that point must make an Agility Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take 3d12 magic damage. Targets who succeed take half damage.

features.name: I'm Speaking
features.description: When a PC interrupts the Supervillain's monologue, you can mark a Stress to seal their mouth closed. They can't speak or cast spells until they end the effect by marking 2 Stress.
features.trigger: When a PC interrupts the Supervillain's monologue

features.name: Maniacal Laugh
features.description: When the Supervillain deals Major or greater damage, you gain a Fear.
features.trigger: When the Supervillain deals Major or greater damage

features.name: Until We Meet Again
features.description: When the Supervillain has marked 7 or more of their HP, you can spend a Fear to have them fly away to a safe location to recover. A PC who succeeds on a Knowledge Roll can trace the Supervillain to their destination.
features.trigger: When the Supervillain has marked 7 or more of their HP

name: Hungering Darkness
description: A black silhouette of a great beast, with glowing eyes and a large maw.

features.name: Shadow Form
features.description: The Darkness is resistant to physical damage. If the Darkness is within Very Close range of bright light, they are Vulnerable.

features.name: Gloom Aura
features.description: When the Darkness makes a successful attack against a PC, they lose a Hope. If they have no Hope, you gain a Fear.

features.name: Envelop
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Melee range. On a success, the Darkness envelops the target and they must mark 2 Stress. The target is Vulnerable to the Darkness, Hidden to other creatures while the Darkness surrounds them, and they must mark a Stress when they act.

features.name: Bring the Darkness
features.description: Mark a Stress to extinguish all non-magical light sources within Far range. Targets in darkness who can't see must mark a Stress and have disadvantage on action rolls. Sheriff At the frontiers, Sheriffs are responsible for enforcing law and order. They solve crimes, keep the peace, and detain and interrogate suspects.

name: Hydra
description: Une bête écailleuse quadrupède à multiples têtes au long cou, chacune remplie de crocs menaçants.

features.name: Many-Headed Menace
features.description: L’Hydre commence avec trois têtes et peut en avoir jusqu’à cinq. Quand l’Hydre subit des dégâts Majeurs ou supérieurs, elle perd une tête.

features.name: Relentless (X)
features.description: L’Hydre peut être mise sous le projecteur X fois par tour du MJ, où X est le nombre de têtes de l’Hydre. Dépensez une Peur comme d’habitude pour la placer sous le projecteur.

features.name: Regeneration
features.description: Si l’Hydre a des PV cochés, retirez un PV et faites pousser deux têtes.

features.name: Terrifying Chorus
features.description: Tous les PJ à Portée Loin perdent 2 Espoirs.

features.name: Magical Weakness
features.description: Quand l’Hydre subit des dégâts magiques, elle devient Étourdie jusqu’au prochain jet avec Peur. Tant qu’Étourdie, elle ne peut pas utiliser son action Régénération mais est immunisée contre les dégâts magiques.
features.trigger: Quand l’hydre subit des dégâts magiques

name: Ignocerous
description: A large, aggressive beast, bathed in fire and brimstone.

features.name: Relentless (3)
features.description: The Ignocerous can be spotlighted up to three times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Wreathed in Flame
features.description: The Ignocerous has resistance to magic damage.

features.name: Blazing Trail
features.description: After the Ignocerous uses its “Fiery Charge”, it leaves a trail of fire along the path it took. PCs must mark a Stress and succeed on an Agility or Strength action roll to move through a trail while it burns, taking 3d4direct damage on a failure. If the Ignocerous takes Severe damage from an attack, all active Blazing Trails subside to embers.

features.name: Fiery Charge
features.description: Spend 2 Fearand the Ignocerous snorts a gout of flame before dashing toward a target within Far range, leaving a “Blazing Trail” in their wake. All targets within Very Close range from where the Ignocerous begins its charge, and those within Melee range of its path to the target, must make an Agility Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take3d6+8magic damage. Targets who succeed must either mark 2 Stress or take half damage. If the Ignocerous succeeds in hitting the target of the charge, that target takes3d20+8physical damage and is knocked back to Close range.

features.name: Infernal Heat
features.description: When a PC damages the Ignocerous from within Very Close range, the PC is subject to a blast of tremendous heat and must make a Strength Reaction Roll. On a failure, they must mark 2 Stress and you gain a Fear. On a success, they must mark 1 Stress.
features.trigger: When a PC damages the Ignocerous from within Very Close range

name: Illumi Paladin
description: An illumi dedicated to an oath, heavily armored and wielding a blade of light.

features.name: Armor Shedding
features.description: The lllumi's Difficulty against attacks is permanently reduced by 1 for each successful attack made against them.

features.name: Angel Wings
features.description: Mark a Stress to fly up to Far range and make a standard attack with advantage against a target.

features.name: Heavenly Judgment
features.description: Spend a Fear to summon a beam of radiant light that shines on a target within Far range. The target must succeed on a Presence Reaction Roll (16) or take 2d10 magic damage. You can mark a Stress to move the light onto a new target within range when the lllumi is spotlighted. I lllumi Paladin Paladins have taken an oath to serve a deity. They are well armored and wield a blade of light and divine powers from their God. lllumi Schemer Schemers will stop at nothing to increase their power. They utilize their small sphere of influence to cause chaos and exploit the opportunities that arise. lllumi Solar Solars have inherent magical abilities as a result of their angelic ancestry.

name: Illumi Schemer
description: A charismatic politician working in a minor position of power, with control over an important resource.

features.name: Spy Network
features.description: Mark a Stress to describe how the Schemer knows a secret about a PC in the scene.

features.name: Instigate
features.description: Spend a Fear to create a disruption within the lllumi's sphere of influence, or spread an existing event into their influence that they can use as justification for consolidating their power, that the PCs are asked to manage. The event might be a riot in a city, an attack on supply lines, or a magical accident, for example.

features.name: Consolidate
features.description: Long-Term Countdown (4). Activate when the lllumi's schemes are first revealed. When it triggers, the lllumi introduces draconian measures to consolidate their power, such as strict curfews, seizing consumables, or restricting magic use.

name: Illumi Solar
description: An illumi with light-based angelic powers.

features.name: Manipulate Magic
features.description: You can mark a Stress to affect a spell the lllumi casts in one of the following ways: •	Increase the range of their standard attack to Far. •	Avoid hitting themselves and allies with their "Blinding Light" action.

features.name: Radiant Orb
features.description: Mark a Stress to create an orb of blinding light within Close range. All creatures within Very Close range must succeed on an Instinct Reaction Roll or take ldl2+6 magic damage and become Vulnerable until they next act.

features.name: Arcane Shove
features.description: When the lllumi takes damage from an attack within Melee range, you can mark a Stress to knock the target back to Far range. Infernis Infernis are descended from the Fallen who reside in the Circles Below. Infernis have devilish horns growing from their heads.
features.trigger: When the lllumi takes damage from an attack within Melee range

name: Inferni Diabolist
description: An inferni adept at summoning demons.

features.name: Sulfurous Aura
features.description: PCs who make an action roll within Melee range of the Inferni must mark a Stress.

features.name: Binding Circle
features.description: Mark a Stress and choose a point within Close range. A circle of magical glyphs appears at Very Close range of that point. All creatures within that area can make an Agility Reaction Roll, moving out of the circle on a success. Creatures who fail can't leave the circle until a creature or object outside the circle moves into the circle. The circle disappears if the Inferni takes Severe damage or moves beyond Far range of the circle.

features.name: Hellfire Burns
features.description: When the Inferni deals Major or greater damage, the target is Ignited until they're extinguished with a successful Finesse Roll. While Ignited, the PC must mark a HP when they make an action roll.
features.trigger: When the Inferni deals Major or greater damage

features.name: Summoning Ritual
features.description: Countdown (4). When the Inferni is in the spotlight for the first time, activate the countdown. When it triggers, summon a Tier 3 Demon.
features.trigger: Countdown (4)

name: Jelloon
description: A giant jellyfish-like creature that floats in the air.

features.name: Stunning Shock
features.description: Mark a Stress to make a standard attack against all targets within Close range. Targets you succeed against must mark a Stress and can't move when they next act.

features.name: Deadly Neurotoxin
features.description: Spend a Fear and make an attack against all targets Restrained by thejelloon. Targets you succeed against take 3d4+10 magic damage. If they mark HP, they become Vulnerable until their next rest.

features.name: Entangle
features.description: When thejelloon makes a successful standard attack, you can mark a Stress to temporarily Restrain the target in their tentacles.
features.trigger: When thejelloon makes a successful standard attack

features.name: Deadly Burst
features.description: When the Jelloon marks their last HP, deal 1d2+10 magic damage to all creatures within Very Close range. K Kaiju Kaiju are gigantic creatures of legend. Their steps shake trees and buildings. Their sighting heralds impeding disaster, causing towns to evacuate and wildlife to flee. They carve out new landscapes as they migrate across the land to destination unknown, impeded by neither nature nor manmade structures. Just hope that a Kaiju is not heading in your direction. Kaiju are the creations of a mad wizard who stole the secrets of crystalmancy from the reclusive subterranean rock-folk known as Terrans (see later in this book). They embedded infused gemstones into mundane beasts, growing them to enormous size and imbuing them with great power. Now set loose on the world, the Kaiju travel slowly and steadily across the land, destroying all in their path. One does not simply fight a Kaiju. When attacked directly, their wounds magically close and heal, so they cannot be defeated like a standard adversary. Instead, efforts should be expended attacking the magical crystals embedded into their body that caused them to grow to their colossal size. Thus, fighting Kaiju is more akin to traversing dangerous terrain: PCs will need to scale the Kaiju’s body or even traverse their insides to find and destroy the crystals, ultimately reverting the Kaiju back into the animal it was before. The Kaiju stat blocks are slightly modified compared to the standard adversary stat blocks. Each Kaiju stat block notes the HP and thresholds for each crystal rather than the Kaiju itself. The Kaiju also does not have any Stress that can be marked. Kaiju encounters are intended as a singular boss fight without the need for any additional adversaries. However, there could be environment actions that suit the Kaiju, or a countdown (e.g. countdown 20) for the PCs to defeat the Kaiju before it starts rampaging through a beloved settlement. The Kaiju stat blocks are accompanied with a map indicating the body parts of the Kaiju, the location of the crystals, and how the body parts are connected. In some cases, crystals are visible from the ground and can be attacked at range, but in others the PCs will have to scale the Kaiju’s body to reach a crystal. To scale the body, the map indicates the suggested ability traits to roll, and the Difficulty required to traverse from the ground to a body part, or from one body part to an adjacent one. Two-way arrows indicate movement can occur in both directions, whereas one-way arrows indicate only movement in one direction is possible, for example representing jumping from a higher body part to a lower one. The Behemoth The Behemoth is an elephantine Kaiju resembling a woolly mammoth, that mostly wanders across arctic regions. Their huge tusks can batter through solid ice and rock. The Behemoth’s only visible crystal is the one on their trunk. The crystals on their head and flanks are obscured by fur. Fortunately, the Behemoth’s fur is easy to climb so PCs can scale the Behemoth and search through their fur to locate the hidden crystals.
features.trigger: When the Jelloon marks their last HP

features.name: Kaiju Map
features.description: The Behemoth Head 1 crystal Agility/Finesse (12) Back Agility/Finesse (14) yAgility/Strength (12)^ Agility/Strength (12) Trunk 1 crystal Left Flank 1 crystal Right Flank 1 crystal Agility/Strength (10) Agility/Strength (10) Left Legs ×2 Right Legs ×2

name: Jorogumo
description: A beautiful woman dressed in fine silks who shapeshifts into a bloodthirsty half-human and half-spider abomination.

features.name: Spider Climb
features.description: The Jorogumo can climb across walls or the ceiling and is unhindered by webbing.

features.name: Shapeshift
features.description: Once per scene, mark a Stress to change into a humanoid form and immediately take the spotlight in that form. Replace this stat block with an Inferni Incubus/Succubus or similar Tier 3 or lower Social type stat block, and revert back when the Jorogumo marks their last HP in that form or takes Severe damage.

features.name: Shadow Webs
features.description: Spend a Fear and choose a point within Far range. All targets within Close range of that point must succeed on a Strength Reaction Roll or become Restrained and Vulnerable in shadow webbing until they break free with a successful Strength Roll. A target Restrained by this feature must mark a Stress when they make an action roll.

features.name: Hemotoxin
features.description: When a target marks HP from the Jorogumo's standard attack, you can mark a Stress to Poison the target until they clear HP. While Poisoned the target makes action rolls with disadvantage. Onis are large ogre-like Yokai who are brutish and violent whilst also possessing magical abilities. In positions of power Oni are tyrannical rulers, bringing in demons from the Circles Below as their lieutenants.
features.trigger: When a target marks HP from the Jorogumo's standard attack

name: Kalimari Grenadier
description: A humanoid with the lower body of an octopus, armed with explosives in each tentacle.

features.name: Smoke Grenade
features.description: Mark a Stress to throw a grenade to a point within Close range. Smoke partially obscures sight within Close range of that point until the end of the scene, giving disadvantage on action rolls.

features.name: Double Strike
features.description: When the Kalimari succeeds with a standard attack, you can mark a Stress to take the spotlight a second time this GM turn.
features.trigger: When the Kalimari succeeds with a standard attack

features.name: Detonation
features.description: Countdown (2). When the Kalimari marks their last HP, activate the countdown. When it triggers, their remaining grenades explode and all creatures within Close range must make an Agility Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take 3d20 physical damage. Targets who succeed take half damage. The grenades can be disarmed with a Knowledge Roll (18), ending the countdown on a success.
features.trigger: Countdown (2)

name: Kalimari Oozeslinger
description: A humanoid with the lower body of an octopus, armed with oversized pistols containing brightly colored cartridges.

features.name: Ooze Tokens
features.description: When the Kalimari makes a standard attack, place an Ooze token on this stat block.

features.name: Awaken Oozes
features.description: Spend a Fear to summon a number of Tiny Ooze adversaries equal to the number of Ooze tokens on this stat block. Spotlight the Oozes, then clear all tokens from the stat block.

features.name: Double Strike
features.description: When the Kalimari succeeds with a standard attack, you can mark a Stress to take the spotlight a second time this GM turn. Kalimari Grenadier Grenadiers have multiple types of handheld explosive projectiles at their disposal. Kalimari Oozeslinger Oozeslingers wield oversized pistols filled with bioengineered oozes. Kalimari Seasoned Fighter Seasoned Fighters wield eight weapons, one for each limb.
features.trigger: When the Kalimari succeeds with a standard attack

name: Kalimari Seasoned Fighter
description: A humanoid with the lower body of an octopus, armed with knives in each tentacle.

features.name: Depleted Defenses
features.description: The Kalimari's Difficulty is reduced by 1 after each standard attack made at Very Close or Close range.

features.name: Double Strike
features.description: When the Kalimari succeeds with a standard attack, you can mark a Stress to take the spotlight a second time this GM turn. Katari Katari are humanoids with feline characteristics. They are fiercely independent which can make them fickle allies. Katari societies are a sprawl of crude dwellings where the Katari climb across buildings just as much as taking the streets. Katari Bounty Hunter Bounty Hunters are rugged felines who take on contracts to bring in wanted criminals.
features.trigger: When the Kalimari succeeds with a standard attack

name: Katari Pit Fighter
description: A brutal kata ri fighter with the feline features of a big cat who fights in front of a crowd.

features.name: Beastly Power
features.description: When you initially roll damage, if you roll the maximum value on a die, also roll a new die of that type and add it to the total.

features.name: Taunt
features.description: A target within Far range must mark a Stress and make a Presence Reaction Roll. On a failure, they have disadvantage on their next attack roll.

features.name: Pounce
features.description: Mark a Stress to leap to a point within Close range. Make an attack with advantage against all targets within Melee range of that point. Targets the Katari succeeds against take 3d6 + 3 physical damage.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Katari makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.
features.trigger: When the Katari makes a successful attack against a PC

features.name: Nine Lives
features.description: When the Katari would mark their last HP and has unmarked Stress, mark all remaining Stress to remain on 1 HP, becoming Vulnerable. Immediately spotlight the Katari.
features.trigger: When the Katari would mark their last HP and has unmarked Stress

name: Kikimora
description: A silent presence in forgotten homes, the Kikimora slips through cracks and weaves sleep into snares. She punishes the guilty with dread and feeds on disorder, grief, and shame.

features.name: Silken Kill
features.description: When the Kikimora succeeds on a standard attack that has advantage, they deal 4d8+6 direct magical damage instead of their standard damage.

features.name: Ghost
features.description: The Kikimora has resistance to physical damage. Mark a Stress to move up to Close range through solid objects.

features.name: Your Struggle Is Pointless
features.description: Spend a Fear to weigh down the spirits of all PCs within Far range. All targets affected replace their Hope Die with a d8 until they roll a success with Hope or their next rest.

features.name: Thin as Dust
features.description: When the Kikimora is hit by an attack, you may mark a Stress to become Hidden until the end of your next turn. Attacks made while Hidden from this feature have advantage.

name: Lamplight Beguiler
description: A huge mer-creature who lures unwary prey into eating range with their dangling, shapeshifting head stalk.
motives: speak through their shapeshifted head stalk

features.name: Doppel-Dangler
features.description: The membranous cluster at the end of the Beguiler's head stalk can shapeshift into any humanoid form. Mark a Stress and choose a PC within Close range. Their player must tell you what form their PC would find most trustworthy. The Beguiler's head stalk transforms into that shape.

features.name: Entice
features.description: Spend a Fear to play an alluring overture to a PC within Close range. The PC must succeed on a Presence Reaction Roll (18) or move into Melee range with the Beguiler. If the Beguiler's head stalk is in a form the PC would find trustworthy, they have disadvantage on the reaction roll.

features.name: Entangle
features.description: When a PC moves within Melee range, you can spend a Fear to make an attack against them. On a successful attack, the target is Swallowed. While Swallowed, a creature is Restrained and can only take actions to attack the Beguiler. The Beguiler disgorges all Swallowed creatures when they take Major damage.
features.trigger: When a PC moves within Melee range

features.name: Rest and Digest
features.description: When the Beguiler takes the spotlight with a Swallowed creature inside them, they move to Far range away from the PCs.
features.trigger: When the Beguiler takes the spotlight with a Swallowed creature inside them

name: Large Nesting
description: A large mass of writhing tentacles.

features.name: Grapple
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Very Close range. On a success, the target is Restrained and Vulnerable until they break free with a successful Strength Roll or the Nesting takes Major or greater damage.

features.name: Fling
features.description: When the Nesting has Restrained a target, mark a Stress to throw them at another target within Close range, ending the condition. Make an attack roll against the new target. On a success, deal 2d12+6 physical damage to both targets.

features.name: Nesting Forms (Phase Change)
features.description: When the Nesting marks their last HP, a Medium Nesting emerges from their corpse and is immediately spotlighted. O Ogre Cannoneer Ogres are large and brutish but dim-witted creatures that make up heavy hitters in war bands. They often work for more intelligent allies such as giants who are still strong enough to keep the Ogres in line. Ogre Cannoneers are equipped with large cannons that they use to deal devastating damage to their foes.
features.trigger: When the Nesting marks their last HP

name: Leshy
description: A trickster spirit of the forest, the Leshy bends paths, commands beasts, and punishes those who disrespect the wild.

features.name: Trickster Tree
features.description: Before they make their first attack in a fight or after they become Hidden, the Leshy is indistinguishable from other trees until they next act or a PC succeeds on an Instinct Roll to identify them. While Hidden, the Leshy attacks with advantage.

features.name: Shrink
features.description: Shrink to the size of a small plant and become Hidden.

features.name: Grow
features.description: Mark a Stress to grow to the size of a great tree. The Leshy can use their Branch Strike to attack targets up to Very Close range and gains +5 to all damage thresholds.

features.name: Wrath of Bark and Root
features.description: While Grown, spend a Fear to make an attack against all targets within Very Close range. Targets the Leshy succeeds against take 3d6+6 physical damage and become Restrained.

features.name: Call of the Wild Ones
features.description: Twice per scene, mark a Stress to summon allies from the forest: Choose to summon three Tier 1, two Tier 2, or one Tier 3 animal or plant adversaries. The summoned creatures must be Bruiser, Skulk, or Standard types and are under the Leshy's control. The adversaries appear at Close range and are immediately spotlighted.

name: Masked Queen
description: A masked warrior that leads groups of bandits.

features.name: Magical Cunning
features.description: The Queen is resistant to magic damage.

features.name: Merciless (1)
features.description: When the Queen is spotlighted, spotlight one additional ally without requiring a Fear to be spent to do so.

features.name: Dirty Fighting
features.description: When the Queen attacks a Vulnerable target they must mark a Stress.

features.name: Never Alone
features.description: Twice per scene, mark a Stress to summon a Veiled Sister who is Hidden within Close range of a PC.

features.name: Hobbling Strike
features.description: Make a standard attack against a target. On a success, Spend a Fear to deal 3d12+5 direct physical damage and Hobble the target until they clear 1 HP. A Hobbled target is Vulnerable and can only move within Very Close range as their normal movement.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Queen makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.
features.trigger: When the Queen makes a successful attack against a PC

name: Master Elementalist
description: A powerful sorcerer drawing upon the elements, clad in robes from different regions representing their travel and studies.

features.name: Wings of Air
features.description: Master Elementalist can fly up to a Far range as part of their action.

features.name: Crashing Waters
features.description: Spend a Fearto force all targets within Close range to make a Strength Reaction Roll or take3d10physical damage and be knocked back to Far range.

features.name: Darting Flames
features.description: Mark a Stressto make an Elemental Blast attack against1d4+2targets within range.

features.name: Earthen Armor
features.description: When the Elementalist suffers damage,mark a Stressto encase themselves in rock armor and suffer half damage.
features.trigger: When the Elementalist suffers damage

name: Maven of Despair
description: An ancient metal and stone statue brought to life, its divinity shattered, but its purpose retained.

features.name: Feelings of Loss
features.description: Targets who mark 2 HP from the Maven of Despair must make a Strength Reaction Roll or be disarmed.

features.name: Cut to the Heart
features.description: Mark a Stress and make a standard attack. On a success, deal 3d10+4 direct physical damage and make the target temporarily Vulnerable.

features.name: Tides of Despair
features.description: Spend a Fear to make an attack against all targets within Close range. All targets the Maven of Despair succeeds against take 3d8 magic damage and are knocked back to Far range.

name: Maven of Grief
description: An ancient metal and stone statue brought to life, its divinity shattered, but its purpose retained.

features.name: Grief-Stricken
features.description: Targets who mark HP from the Maven of Grief’s attacks are Vulnerable until they clear 1 HP.

features.name: Painful Memory
features.description: Spend a Fear. The Maven of Grief emits a keening wail at all targets in front of them up to a Close range. Targets must succeed on an Instinct Reaction Roll or take 4d8+6 magic damage. Targets that are Vulnerable have disadvantage on the roll.

features.name: Leave Me With My Grief
features.description: When a PC within Very Close range misses with an attack on the Maven of Grief, mark a Stress to make a standard attack against the PC and then move anywhere within Close range.
features.trigger: When a PC within Very Close range misses with an attack on the Maven of Grief

name: Maven of Regret
description: An ancient metal and stone statue brought to life, its divinity shattered, but its purpose retained.

features.name: Ramp up
features.description: You must spend a Fear to spotlight the Maven of Regret. While spotlighted, they can make their standard attack against all targets within range.

features.name: Flagellant
features.description: When the Maven of Regret marks 2 or fewer HP from an attack, you gain a Fear.

features.name: Divine Punishment
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Far range. On a success, the target takes 3d10+8 magic damage and is Restrained by chains of regret until they break free with a successful Strength or Agility Roll(15). If the target is above the Maven of Regret, they have advantage on this attack.

features.name: Sacred Regret
features.description: When the Maven of Regret misses all targets of an attack, Mark a Stress to reroll the attack.
features.trigger: When the Maven of Regret misses all targets of an attack

name: Maven of Sorrow
description: An ancient metal and stone statue brought to life, its divinity shattered, but its purpose retained.

features.name: Heavy Hearted
features.description: Targets who take damage from the Maven of Sorrow have disadvantage on their next action roll..

features.name: Strike of Emptiness
features.description: Make a standard attack. On a success, mark a Stress to force the target to place a domain card of their choice with a Recall cost of 1 or more from their loadout into their vault. If they cannot they lose all Hope.

features.name: Shared Sorrow
features.description: When the Maven of Sorrow marks HP from an attack, spend a number of Fear equal to the HP marked by the Maven of Sorrow to cause the attacker to mark the same number of HP.
features.trigger: When the Maven of Sorrow marks HP from an attack

name: Medium Nesting
description: A medium mass of writhing tentacles.

features.name: Grow (Phase Change)
features.description: If there are 5 tokens on the stat block, mark a Stress to turn the Nesting into a Large Nesting. Clear the tokens, but roll over the number of marked HP and Stress onto the new stat block.

features.name: Fling
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Close range. On a success, the Nesting flings a piece of dead Nesting or other object at the target, dealing 2dlO physical damage.

features.name: Cultivate Mass
features.description: When a PC rolls with Fear, you can add the Fear gained to the stat block instead of your Fear pool.
features.trigger: When a PC rolls with Fear

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Nesting makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear, which you can choose to add to the stat block.
features.trigger: When the Nesting makes a successful attack against a PC

features.name: Nesting Forms (Phase Change)
features.description: When the Nesting marks their last HP, a Small Nesting emerges from their corpse and is immediately spotlighted. The only way to truly defeat a Nesting is to defeat the smallest form so that no further Nestings can emerge from its corpse. To run a Nesting encounter, spend 2 battle points (BPs) to start with a Small Nesting, 6 for a Medium Nesting, and 11 for a Large Nesting.
features.trigger: When the Nesting marks their last HP

name: Megalodon
description: An enormous shark that seeks out wounded creatures in the water to make its prey.

features.name: Aquatic Hunter
features.description: The Megalodon canmark a Stressto add their Experience to an attack roll.

features.name: Overwhelming
features.description: When the Megalodon makes a successful attack, all PCs within Close range mark a Stress and you gain a Fear.

features.name: Blood Rage
features.description: If a PC within Close range has marked half or more of its HP, the Megalodon has disadvantage on all other targets.

features.name: Ramming Bite
features.description: Mark a Stressto move up to Far range before making a standard attack. If successful, the damage is direct physical damage.

name: Mentalist
description: A person with augmented mental capabilities.

features.name: Sharp Mind
features.description: The Mentalist can't be forced to mark Stress.

features.name: Calculated
features.description: Mark a Stress to deal the Mentalist's standard attack damage to a target within Close range.

features.name: Two Can Play That Game
features.description: Mark a Stress to use the same spell as a PC that the Mentalist took the spotlight from. When relevant, spend or gain Fear instead of Hope.

name: Monarch
description: Le souverain d’une nation, auréolé du privilège de la tradition et maniant un pouvoir inégalé dans son domaine.

features.name: Execute Them!
features.description: Faites condamner le groupe pour des crimes réels ou imaginaires. Un PJ qui réussit un jet de Présence peut exiger un procès par combat ou une autre forme spéciale de procès.

features.name: Crownsguard
features.description: Une fois par scène, invoquez six Minions de Tier 3, qui apparaissent à Portée Proche pour faire respecter la volonté du Monarque.

features.name: Casus Belli
features.description: Compte à rebours à long terme (8). Dépensez une Peur pour l’activer après que le désir de guerre du Monarque soit révélé pour la première fois. Quand il se déclenche, le Monarque a une raison de rallier la nation à la guerre. Vous gagnez 1d4 Peurs.
features.trigger: Quand il se déclenche, le monarque a une raison de rallier la nation à la guerre

name: Murder Mystery
description: An ally of the PCs was murdered, and it is up to the PCs to find the culprit.

features.name: Building the Mystery
features.description: An ally or acquaintance of the PCs has just been murdered. The suspects are at the murder scene under guard. Roll a d4 or choose from the following prompts to build the scenario. •	Victim: What career did the victim have? 1. guild or trade, 2. magical or academic, 3. religious or cultural, 4. political or investigative. •	Location: Where did the murder take place? 1. private manor, 2. job site, 3. public building, 4. mode of transport. •	Means: How was the victim killed? 1. physical attack, 2. elemental attack, 3. magical attack, 4. poison. •	Motive: Why was the victim murdered? 1. steal an artifact, 2. seek revenge, 3. silence a secret, 4. gain more power. •	Opportunity: How did the murderer attack without notice? 1. created a distraction, 2. lured victim away, 3. knew the victim's routine, 4. waited for an opportunistic moment. Did a guard invite the PCs or are they suspects? Did the victim say anything cryptic to the PCs while they were alive?

features.name: Uncovering Clues
features.description: PCs can interrogate suspects, search for clues on the victim or at the crime scene, or otherwise use their abilities to narrow down suspects until only the killer remains. Who found the body? Who are the prime suspects? Is there a twist, such as the body being moved, the murderer being charmed by the true killer, or two suspects working together?

features.name: Interrogation
features.description: A suspect is available for interrogation. Each suspect has the means to carry out the murder, but it might be inconsistent with the manner of death. Each suspect has a connection to the victim that can be revealed with a successful Presence Roll and might be a motive for murder. Some will have had the opportunity to murder that can be revealed when the time of death is established. Other clues that can emerge from an interrogation might include their suspicious behavior, gossip about others, physical evidence on their body or clothing, or items in their possession. Do any of the suspects have contradictory alibis? Do any of the suspects have unrelated secrets that make them act suspiciously? Were any of the suspects recently injured?

features.name: Finale
features.description: During the reveal, spend a Fear to have the murderer take the spotlight and attack or try to flee. Was the murder part of a larger plot?

name: Mutagenist Sister
description: A humanoid in a heavy coat with several syringes and flasks

features.name: Powerful Shot
features.description: Inject an ally within Melee range with a powerful formula. The ally marks a Stress. Until the ally takes Severe damage they gain a +5 bonus to their damage.

features.name: Cruel Shot
features.description: Inject an ally within Melee range with a cruel formula. The ally marks a Stress and have a shot Countdown (3). It ticks down each time the ally is spotlighted. While the countdown is active, when the ally makes a successful attack, you gain a Fear.

features.name: Taste of My Own Medicine
features.description: Spend a Fear to inject the Mutagenist with an experimental formula, becoming Mutated. While Mutated they gain the following benefits: • Their Thresholds and attack rolls gain a bonus equal to their highest experience. • They can only make standard attacks and must mark a Stress to do so. • Their standard attacks do 3d12+4 magic damage Once the Mutagenist marks their last stress, they make one final standard attack then are no longer Mutated.

name: Mutater Hag
description: An elderly crone with large daws, capable of altering faces and memories.

features.name: Coven
features.description: Increase the Hag's Difficulty by 1 for each Hag within Far range.

features.name: Warp Terrain
features.description: Mark a Stress to liquify the ground at a point within Close range. All targets within Very Close range of that point must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll or become Restrained and Vulnerable until they break free with a successful Strength Roll.

features.name: Alter Ancestry
features.description: Spend a Fear to attempt to mutate a target's body within Close range. The target must succeed on an Instinct Reaction Roll or begin to turn into a different ancestry, starting a Transformation Countdown (4). The countdown ticks down each time the Hag is attacked. When triggered, the target instantly transforms and the new ancestry replaces their existing ancestry. If the Hag is defeated, all transformation countdowns end.

name: Naga Empress
description: A huge snake with a humanoid face that rules over a colony of nagas.

features.name: Terrifying
features.description: When the Empress makes a successful attack, all PCs within Far range lose a Hope and you gain a Fear. Relentless (3): The Empress can be spotlighted up to three times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Venom Spit
features.description: Mark a Stress and make an attack against all targets within Close range in front of the Naga. Targets the Naga succeeds against take 3d6+5 magic damage.

features.name: Curse of Nagafication
features.description: Spend a Fear to force a target to make an Instinct Reaction Roll. On a failure, they begin to turn into a Naga, starting a Transformation Countdown (4). The countdown ticks down each time the Empress is attacked. When triggered, the target instantly transforms and the Naga ancestry (this book) replaces their ancestry. If the Empress is defeated, all transformation countdowns end.

features.name: Swarm of Snakes
features.description: Once per scene, spend a Fear to summon 1d4 Swarm of Snakes within Far range (reflavor Electric Eels (SRD), with the "Paralyzing Shock" action renamed Venomous Bites). You can spend a Fear to spotlight all the Swarm of Snakes.

features.name: Snake Eyes
features.description: When a PC rolls a critical success, you can mark 2 Stress to turn it into a critical failure. The target fails the attack, must mark a Stress, and you gain a Fear.
features.trigger: When a PC rolls a critical success

name: Naga Oracle
description: A half-snake, half-human hybrid armed with two curved blades.

features.name: Foresight
features.description: The Naga can choose the target of their standard attack after making their attack roll.

features.name: Shed Skin
features.description: Once per scene, mark a Stress to end a temporary condition.

features.name: Constrict
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Very Close range. On a success, deal 3d6+8 direct physical damage and the target must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll or become temporarily Restrained by the Naga's tail. You can mark a Stress to deal an additional 1d20 direct physical damage each time the Naga acts.

features.name: Serpent-Entwined Bracelet
features.description: Once per scene, mark a HP to clear 1d4 HP on an ally within Close range.

name: Naga Shapeshifter
description: A humanoid with a snake head capable of altering their appearance. Motives & Tactics: Infiltrate settlements, poison leaders, kidnap powerful

features.name: Preserve for the Empress
features.description: When a target of the Naga's attacks marks their last HP, they fall unconscious for 1d4 hours or until they regain HP, instead of making a death move.

features.name: Shed Skin
features.description: Once per scene, mark a Stress to end a temporary condition.

features.name: Shapeshift
features.description: Once per scene, mark a Stress to transform into a Giant Snake or a Tier 1 or 2 humanoid of any Ancestry and immediately take the spotlight in that form. Use the new stat block instead of this one, reverting back when the Naga marks their last HP in that form or takes Severe damage.

features.name: Naga Poison
features.description: When the Naga deals Major or greater damage, you can spend a Fear to Poison the target until they clear HP. While Poisoned, they make attack rolls with disadvantage. Nature Guardians Nature Guardians are worldly beings that guard over a biome. They are known by enigmatic titles such as “the Titan of the Mountain” or “the Lake Spirit”, and some people pray to the Nature Guardians as minor deities. Nature Guardians present a trial to PCs to assess their capabilities before lending aid. Nature Guardians take responsibility for the land and so confront forces of evil head-on to defend it. If they fail this task or miss an important detail, the Nature Guardian can become corrupted by the evil, and the surrounding land becomes corrupted with them. Baihu Baihu are tiger-like humanoids with white fur who are guardians of a jungle. Demonic or necromantic incursions into the jungle might corrupt a Baihu. A Baihu might present an obstacle course trial involving climbing trees, swinging from vines, and fording rivers. Dragon Turtle A Dragon Turtle is a huge turtle who is guardian of an ocean. The overgrown moss on their shell can be mistaken for an island at a distance. Infected plants on their shell might cause a Dragon Turtle to be corrupted over time. A Dragon Turtle’s trial might involve transporting PCs below the ocean’s surface to a reef, then tasking them with surviving the return to the surface – too far to just swim – without air to breathe. The tools for survival and pockets of air can be found within the reef. Naiad Naiads are humanoid guardians of a lake. Pollution of the lake might corrupt a Naiad. Naiads sing to the water to control it. A Naiad’s trial might involve memory, such as tasking the PCs to jump across lily pads to reach an island at the center of the lake, where only certain lily pads can support their weight, requiring they learn the correct combination or otherwise identify which lily pads are the supportive ones. Roc Rocs are huge birds who are guardians of a mountain. A Roc might become corrupted if an adversary kidnaps their chick, or from a force of darkness building within the mountain. A Roc’s trial might involve a leap of faith, such as building a glider and launching off the top of the mountain to reach an otherwise inaccessible point. Sphinx Sphinxes are winged lion-like creatures who are guardians of a desert. A Sphinx might become corrupted by a force of darkness long buried beneath the desert dunes. A Sphinx’s trial might involve answering riddles whilst under attack from monsters.
features.trigger: When the Naga deals Major or greater damage

name: Naiad
description: A female humanoid with pale blue skin, watching over a lake.

features.name: Lily Pads
features.description: When the Naiad moves across water, they leave behind lily pads that can support a PC's weight.

features.name: Sing to the Water
features.description: Mark 2 Stress to summon a Greater Water Elemental (SRD).

features.name: Water Wall
features.description: Mark a Stress to create a wall of water between two points within Far range. Ranged attacks through the wall are made with disadvantage.

features.name: Lily Trap
features.description: When a PC moves onto a lily pad, spend a Fear to have it close around them and pull them under the water's surface. The target becomes temporarily Restrained and must mark a Stress as they begin drowning.
features.trigger: When a PC moves onto a lily pad

features.name: Nature's Karma
features.description: When a creature within Far range uses an area of effect attack or otherwise harms the environment, deal 2d10 magic damage to the attacker and you gain a Fear.
features.trigger: When a creature within Far range uses an area of effect attack or otherwise harms the environment

name: Oak Treant
description: Un vieux chêne animé robuste et ancien.
motives: Se cacher à la vue de tous

features.name: Just a Tree
features.description: Avant sa première attaque ou après être devenu Caché, le Tréant est indiscernable des autres arbres jusqu’à ce qu’il agisse ou qu’un PJ réussisse un jet d’Instinct.

features.name: Seed Barrage
features.description: Faites une attaque contre jusqu’à trois cibles à Portée Proche. Les cibles touchées subissent 2d10+5 dégâts physiques.

features.name: Take Root
features.description: Enracinez le Tréant sur place. Le Tréant est Entravé tant qu’il est Enraciné, et peut mettre fin à cet effet au lieu de se déplacer. Tant qu’Enraciné, le Tréant a une résistance aux dégâts physiques.

name: Omara, the Grave Hallowed
description: A shadow-cursed vampire, once touched by the divine and left to languish, feral and enraged

features.name: Relentless (3)
features.description: Omara can be spotlighted up to three times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Grave Hallowed
features.description: Omara critically succeeds on a 19 or 20. Additionally, when she critically succeeds, you gain a Fear and Omara clears a Stress.

features.name: Blood Hungry
features.description: Omara has advantage on attacks against targets that have marked HP.

features.name: Raking Claws
features.description: Mark a Stress and make a standard attack against all targets within Close range. Targets Omara succeeds against mark a number of Stress equal to the number of HP they marked from this attack and you gain a Fear.

features.name: Feel my Fury
features.description: Spend a Fear to send crystalized blood out in all directions. All targets within Close range must make an Agility Reaction Roll or take 2d10+6 direct physical damage. Targets who succeed take half damage. Omara can only use this feature once per GM Turn.

features.name: Terrifying Howl
features.description: Once per Scene, Mark a Stress to make all targets within Far range lose a Hope. If a target is not able to lose 2 Hope, they must instead mark 2 Stress.

name: Omara, the Shadow Cursed
description: A sanguine hunter of the shadows who calls death her friend. She is dressed in dark clothes and red armor

features.name: Relentless (2)
features.description: Omara can be spotlighted up to two times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Shadow Touched
features.description: Omara critically succeeds on a 19 or 20.

features.name: Lightly Armored
features.description: When Omara takes physical damage, reduce it by 4.

features.name: Blood-Drenched Blade
features.description: Make a standard attack against a creature in range. On a success, mark a Stress to empower Omara’s blade. It does 3d12 +6 magical damage and you gain a Fear. Omara can only use this feature once per GM Turn.

features.name: Shadow Rush
features.description: Spend a Fear to teleport to a point within Far range. Make an attack at advantage against a target within Melee Range. On a success, the target takes 2d20 physical damage and must make a Strength Reaction Roll. On a failure the target is knocked back to a point within Far range.

features.name: You Cannot Win
features.description: Spend 2 Fear to feast on the blood of the wounded. All targets within Close range must make a Strength or Agility Reaction Roll. Targets who have marked HP have disadvantage. Targets who fail mark 1 HP. All targets lose a Hope and Omara clears 1 HP.

features.name: Feed Me Your Failure
features.description: When a PC rolls with Fear within Close range of Omara, they must mark a Stress and you gain an additional Fear.
features.trigger: When a PC rolls with Fear within Close range of Omara

features.name: Hateful Vow (Phase Change)
features.description: When Omara marks her last HP, replace her with Omara, The Grave Hallowed Cerberus
features.trigger: When Omara marks her last HP

name: Oni
description: An ogre-like malevolent shapeshifting spirit.

features.name: Bone Breaker
features.description: The Oni's physical attacks deal direct damage, including while shapechanged.

features.name: Shapeshift
features.description: Once per scene, mark a Stress to change into a humanoid form and immediately take the spotlight in that form. Replace this stat block with a Tier 3 or lower humanoid adversary, and revert back when the Oni marks their last HP in that form or takes Severe damage.

features.name: Suffocate
features.description: Mark a Stress and choose a point within Far range. All targets within Very Close range of that point must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll (16) or become trapped in a bubble that contains no air. Targets who fail are Restrained and Vulnerable in the bubble until they break free with a successful Agility or Strength Roll (16). Targets Restrained in this way must mark a Stress when a PC makes an action roll.

features.name: Mind Scramble
features.description: Mark a Stress to inflict mental anguish on the minds of nearby foes. All targets within Close range must make an Instinct Reaction Roll. For each target who failed you gain a Fear and the Oni knows one of the target's fears.

features.name: Magical Bombardment
features.description: Spend a Fear and make an attack against all targets within Far range in front of the Oni. Targets the Oni succeeds against take 3d10+6 magic damage. Z Zaxian A Zaxian is a flying tentacled being. Using their tentacles, they can pull on the threads of reality, shifting the world around them between two and three dimensions. When shifted to two dimensions, depth is reduced down to only a few feet. For a Zaxian encounter, use a 2D side-on battle map. Obstacles with a width greater than this new depth take up the whole depth, and must be climbed over or under or destroyed to move past them. Small obstacles such as other PCs maintain their depth and can be squeezed by. Passageways leading into the third dimension can become blocked by a shift to two dimensions. As such, Zaxians are sometimes trained to be guardians of treasures or other secrets, since they can seal off access to restricted areas until they are defeated.

name: Orc Nemesis
description: An angry orc looking to trash the reputation of their rivals.

features.name: Rivalry
features.description: The Nemesis and the PCs become enemies when the PCs threaten, undermine, or financially harm the Nemesis. When the Nemesis is Vulnerable a PC can end the rivalry with a successful Presence Roll.

features.name: Disrupt
features.description: Mark a Stress to try to outdo the PCs, for example hiring rival adventurers for a quest or undercutting the PCs' business.

features.name: Sabotage
features.description: Mark a Stress and spend a Fear to dispatch 1d4+l adversaries to sabotage the PCs, such as to steal from them, trash their accommodation, or beat them up.

features.name: Mind Games
features.description: When the PCs are within Close range of the Nemesis, you can mark a Stress to change a roll with Hope to one with Fear. P Phantom Phantoms are adversaries spawned from a nightmare. They take the appearance of an upcoming threat, and a PC might face a Phantom during a dream or vision or after waking. Phantoms are not powerful, but they might be faced by PCs without armor and weapons readily available. They are also effective at rebuilding expended Hope and Fear pools before a big confrontation.
features.trigger: When the PCs are within Close range of the Nemesis

name: Pegasus
description: A majestic, winged horse sometimes used as a mount by powerful beings.

features.name: Flying
features.description: While flying with a rider, the Pegasus grants a +2 bonus to the rider’s Difficulty.

features.name: Flyby
features.description: Mark a Stressand choose a target within Far range to swoop past and make an attack against. On a success, the target takes3d12+2physical damage and is Vulnerableuntil they clear HP. If the Pegasus has a rider, instead of the Pegasus attacking, the rider makes an attack against the target with advantage.

features.name: Spur On
features.description: When the Pegasus’s rider makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.
features.trigger: When the Pegasus’s rider makes a successful attack against a PC

name: Phoenix
description: A large bird made of elemental energy.

features.name: Flyby
features.description: While flying, the Phoenix moves up to Close range after making a standard attack.

features.name: Flare
features.description: Mark a Stress to expel elemental energy from the Phoenix's body. All targets within Very Close range must make an Agility Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take 2d12+2 magic damage and gain an Element token from residual elements surrounding their body, which can be cleared with a Finesse Roll (14).

features.name: Adverse Weather
features.description: Countdown (Loop 1d6). Mark 2 Stress to create adverse weather conditions, such as a blizzard, fire tornado, or lightning storm. Activate the countdown, which ticks down when a PC rolls with Fear or by spending a Fear. When it triggers, all PCs must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll or take 4d6+4 direct magic damage. PCs with an Element token have disadvantage on this roll.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Phoenix makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.
features.trigger: When the Phoenix makes a successful attack against a PC

features.name: Regenerate
features.description: When the Phoenix marks their last HP, they explode with elemental energy, dealing 3d6 magic damage to all creatures within Very Close range and leaving behind a Phoenix Hatchling egg which hatches after a long rest. Phoenix Fire Phoenixes are made of flame and are reborn from their ashes, while Ice Phoenixes are made of ice and are reborn from their melting body, and Thunder Birds are made of lightning and are reborn from the dissipating clouds of their body. Phoenix Hatchling A Phoenix Hatchling is the newborn chick formed when a Phoenix is reborn. With training, Phoenix Hatchlings can learn to fly and aid in battle.
features.trigger: When the Phoenix marks their last HP

name: Priest of Decay
description: A fungril sworn to the power of decay, preaching the inevitability of death for all things. Priest of Decay

features.name: Doctrine of the End
features.description: The Priest and all allies within Close range deal an additional1d12 damage toVulnerabletargets.

features.name: Life From Death
features.description: Spend a Fearto target a corpse or dead plant within Far range. Create1d4 Treant Saplings at the location of the target.

features.name: We’re All Dying Inside
features.description: Mark a Stressand select a target within Far range. The target must make a Strength Reaction Roll. On a failure, the target marks a HP and becomesVulnerableuntil they clear HP.

features.name: Psalm of Inevitability
features.description: When a PC within Close range of the Priest fails a roll, they must mark a Stress.
features.trigger: When a PC within Close range of the Priest fails a roll

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When this adversary makes a successful attack against a PC, gain a Fear.
features.trigger: When this adversary makes a successful attack against a PC

name: Primordial Basilisk
description: A six-legged lizard the size of a small house with strong scales and hundreds of spikes on its hide.

features.name: Stonewither Spores
features.description: The Basilisk’s body is covered with Stonewither Spores that can turn living material to stone. When a PC has 3 or more petrification tokens, they areRestrained. If a target has 6 or more tokens they must make a death move. A petrification token can be removed with a successful Finesse Roll (16 + the number of petrification tokens). A critical success removes all petrification tokens.

features.name: Diseased Hide
features.description: On an attack within Melee range against the Basilisk, the attacker gains a petrification token.

features.name: Infesting Claws
features.description: Make an attack against a target in Very Close range. On a success,mark a Stressto deal 2d20physical damage and the target gains a petrification token for each HP they mark.

features.name: Petrification Breath
features.description: Spend 2 Fearto release a vile blast of petrifying spores in front of the Basilisk in Far range. Give all targets in the area a petrification token.

features.name: Rampage Spike
features.description: Countdown (Decreasing 4). When the Basilisk takes damage for the first time, activate the countdown. When it triggers, the Basilisk slams its tail on the ground, causing a shockwave. All creatures within Close range must succeed on a Strength Reaction Roll or be knocked to the ground and madeVulnerableuntil they next act. Targets that areRestrainedhave disadvantage on this roll. If the countdown ever decreases its maximum value to 0, the Basilisk does a giant sweep with its tail against all creatures and objects within Close range, dealing2d20direct damage. If a petrified creature is in the area, they are smashed to pieces.
features.trigger: Countdown (Decreasing 4)

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Basilisk makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.
features.trigger: When the Basilisk makes a successful attack against a PC

name: Quetzalcoatlus
description: A winged reptile with huge wingspan and long beak.

features.name: Flying
features.description: While flying, the Dinosaur gains a +3 bonus to their Difficulty.

features.name: Pack Tactics
features.description: If the Dinosaur makes a successful standard attack and another Quetzalcoatlus is within Melee range of the target, deal 3d8 physical damage instead of their standard damage.

features.name: Aerial Drop
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Close range. On a success, mark a Stress to lift the target into the air and drop them within Close range. The target takes 1d2O+5 direct physical damage and is knocked down, becoming Vulnerable until they next act.

features.name: Primeval Instinct
features.description: When the Dinosaur makes a successful standard attack against a Vulnerable target, you can mark a Stress to deal double damage.
features.trigger: When the Dinosaur makes a successful standard attack against a Vulnerable target

name: Reaper
description: A cloaked skeletal figure armed with a scythe.

features.name: Judgment
features.description: Mark a Stress to judge a target. The target must make a Presence Reaction Roll. On a roll with Hope, the target is Worthy, and on a roll with Fear, the target is Unworthy. Gain Hope or Fear as if it were an action roll. On a failure, the target is marked for death and when the Reaper damages the target they deal Severe damage instead of their standard damage. The Reaper can only mark one target at a time.

features.name: Soul Sever
features.description: Spend a Fear and make an attack against a PC within Melee range. On a success, the PC's soul is removed from their body. The PC's body becomes a Shambling Zombie (SRD) under your control and the PC's soul uses the Ghost stat block with no marked HP or Stress (they continue to roll Duality Dice and use their proficiency for damage). The PC can use the Ghost's "Possess" action to reclaim their body if the Shambling Zombie is either still alive or was defeated no more than 24 hours prior. Spirits Spirits are forces of will, separate from a body and soul. By themselves a Spirit exists as a mote of floating magical light that can’t communicate other than through their movement. Frost Spirits Frost Spirits are motes of white light that are born amidst extreme weather in cold regions. They act to destroy any sources of warmth, including from living beings.

name: Rhino Beetle
description: An armored beetle the size of a rhino.

features.name: Thick Hide
features.description: When the Beetle takes physical damage, reduce it by 1d6.

features.name: Burrow
features.description: Mark a Stress to burrow into the ground. When the Beetle next acts, they appear within Far range and can make a standard attack with advantage against all creatures within Very Close range of that point, knocking them back to Close range on a success.

features.name: Charge
features.description: Spend a Fear to move to a point within Close range and make an attack against all targets in the Beetle's path. Targets the Beetle succeeds against take 2d10+2 physical damage and are knocked back to Far range.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Beetle makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear. Swarm of Barnaclemites Barnaclemites are half-barnacle and halftermite and are a huge nuisance to sailors if they attach to a ship, as they can quickly chew through the wood of the hull.
features.trigger: When the Beetle makes a successful attack against a PC

name: Ribbet Leaper Knight
description: An acrobatic armored ribbet wielding a spear.

features.name: Mobile
features.description: After taking an action the Ribbet can leap up to Close range.

features.name: Acrobatic Strike
features.description: Mark a Stress to leap over a target within Very Close range and strike at them from behind. The target must make an Instinct Reaction Roll to track the Ribbet's movement. On a failure, deal 2dlO+2 physical damage and the target's damage thresholds are reduced by 2 until the end of the scene. Ribbet Skulk Skulks take out their foes indirectly from the effects of toxins extracted from their body.

name: Ribbet Skulk
description: A small ribbet that hides in the shadows, using toxin-infused darts to distract their foes.

features.name: Sleep Dart
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Close range. On a success, the target falls Asleep until they take damage, they hear a loud sound, or they mark 2 Stress to wake.

features.name: Rage Dart
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Close range. On a success, the target becomes Enraged. While Enraged, the target must make a Presence Reaction Roll when they act. On a success, they end the condition. On a failure, they attack the nearest creature.

features.name: Tongue Grab
features.description: Mark a Stress and make an attack against a target within Close range. On a success, steal one item or consumable from the target's inventory. You have advantage on this roll if the target is Asleep or Enraged.

features.name: Blend In
features.description: When the Ribbet makes a successful attack, you can mark a Stress to become Hidden until the Ribbet's next attack or a PC succeeds on an Instinct Roll (14) to find them. Riftheart A Riftheart is a huge beating heart with a multitude of arteries and veins extending out from it. The Riftheart can draw in sources of magic and channel them to power rifts to other realms. Their arteries provide the energy to create the rifts, while the veins slither through the rifts to locate and draw in magic sources from the lands beyond. The ability to create rifts makes a Riftheart extremely valuable. An adversary in possession of a Riftheart will endeavor to keep it protected, as they can train a Riftheart to create rifts to other planes of existence on demand, in order to conduct raids on other realms without facing retribution. When a Riftheart is defeated, any rifts they have created immediately begin to close.
features.trigger: When the Ribbet makes a successful attack

name: Riftheart
description: A huge beating heart which powers a rift between realms.

features.name: Interior
features.description: The interior of the Riftheart is immune to magic damage and physical damage is doubled.

features.name: Vein Suction
