features.description: Mark a Stress and make an attack against a target within Far range. On a success, the target is sucked into the Riftheart and Restrained in its interior, taking 2d12+5 direct physical damage. The target must mark a Stress when they act and can break free with a successful Agility Roll.

features.name: Artery Flow
features.description: All targets within the Riftheart must make a Strength Reaction Roll. On a failure, the target is expelled from the Riftheart's interior, and knocked back to Far range.

features.name: Create Rift
features.description: Spend a Fear to create a rift and pull an adversary such as a Mawler or Jelloon through it. Roll a d4. On a 1, a Void Wraith is also summoned. Immediately spotlight the summoned creature(s).

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Riftheart makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear. Rotts A Rott is a force of death, drawing its strength by decaying nature. Rotts suck the color from their surroundings, leaving a landscape of gloomy darkness.
features.trigger: When the Riftheart makes a successful attack against a PC

name: Rolling Eye
description: The remains of a Soul Flower’s eyestalk, which seeks more of its own kind.

features.name: Minion (5)
features.description: The Rolling Eye is defeated when they take any damage. For every 5 damage a PC deals to a Rolling Eye, defeat an additional Minion within range the attack would succeed against.

features.name: Combine and Grow
features.description: If six or more Rolling Eyes are within Close range of each other,spend a Fearto have them move together and become a Shrieking Stalk, which is spotlighted immediately.

features.name: Pop
features.description: When a Rolling Eye is defeated, it pops, dealing 6 physical damage to all targets in Melee range. If more than one Rolling Eye is defeated with the same attack, combine the damage.
features.trigger: When a Rolling Eye is defeated

name: Rott
description: A floating skull surrounded by black wispy tendrils.

features.name: Rotten Aura
features.description: Healing within Very Close range of the Rott only clears half HP (round down).

features.name: Environmental Decay
features.description: Create up to 4 Rottrees from trees within Close range.

features.name: Wither
features.description: Mark a Stress to force all targets within Very Close range to make a Reaction Roll using their highest trait. On a failure, that trait is reduced by 1d4 until they succeed on a roll with that trait or their next rest.

features.name: Monochrome
features.description: Spend a Fear to drain the land of color. All PCs within Close range must make a Presence Reaction Roll. PCs who fail take 3dlO magic damage and can't use Hope features until they roll a success with Hope.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Rott makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear. Rott The Rott has the appearance of a floating skull surrounded by swirling clouds of dark energy. Long black tendrils extend from the skull that blight all that they touch. Rottree Rottrees are blackened trees formed when a tree is infected by decay from a Rott.
features.trigger: When the Rott makes a successful attack against a PC

name: Rottree
description: A warped and blackened awakened tree.

features.name: Rotten Roots
features.description: Mark a Stress to reach out with rotten roots and force all targets within Very Close range to make an Agility Reaction Roll. On a failure, the target is temporarily Restrained by roots and loses a Hope.

features.name: Group Attack
features.description: Spend a Fear to choose a target and spotlight all Rottrees within Very Close range of them. Those Minions make one shared attack roll. On a success, they deal 6 physical damage each. Combine this damage. S Scentipede Scentipedes are giant insects that make excellent tracking animals, as they are able to pick up a days-old scent if presented with an example of that scent. They can skitter towards their target at high speed using a hundred tiny legs.

name: Scythicant Sentinel
description: A corrupted faerie infused with the blood of devils, mindless in its pursuit of trespassers.

features.name: Acid Blood
features.description: On a successful attack within Melee range against the Sentinel, the attacker must mark an Armor Slot without receiving its benefits. If they can’t mark an Armor Slot, they must mark 1 HP.

features.name: Brute
features.description: If the Sentinel rolls a 1 on any die, they can reroll that die.

features.name: Flying Leap
features.description: The Sentinel canmark a Stressto move to a Far range and make a standard attack.

name: Sea Giant
description: A giant who tames underwater beasts.

features.name: Deadly Companion
features.description: Once per scene, summon a Narwhal, Seadragon, or similar Tier 1 or 2 aquatic animal under the Giant's control. The adversary appears at Close range and is immediately spotlighted.

features.name: Waterjet
features.description: Mark a Stress to shoot a jet of chilled water at a target within Close range. Make an attack against the target. On a success, the target takes 2d10+4 magic damage and is Restrained in ice until they break free with a successful Strength Roll.

features.name: Rogue Wave
features.description: Spend a Fear to create a tidal wave in front of the Giant. All targets in the water within Far range must succeed on a Strength Reaction Roll or take 1d20+5 physical damage and be knocked back to Very Far range.

name: Selachi Ghostfin
description: A shark-like humanoid with translucent skin.

features.name: Create Duplicates
features.description: Mark a Stress to create two illusory Duplicates of the Selachi within Close range. Any existing duplicates disappear. Each Duplicate uses the Selachi's standard attack and has a Difficulty of 14 and 1 HP. A PC can identify the true Selachi with a successful Knowledge Roll.

features.name: Pass Through
features.description: When an attacker misses against the Selachi, you can spend a Fear for the attack to pass through the Selachi and damage a different target in the attack's path and within range, dealing half damage.
features.trigger: When an attacker misses against the Selachi

features.name: Abyssal Visions
features.description: When the target of the Selachi's attack marks HP, they must mark the same number of Stress.
features.trigger: When the target of the Selachi's attack marks HP

name: Selachi Megalodon
description: A hulking shark-like humanoid with numerous scars across their body.

features.name: Ramp Up
features.description: You must spend a Fear to spotlight the Selachi. While spotlighted, they can make their standard attack against all targets within range.

features.name: Blood Frenzy
features.description: The Selachi has advantage on attack rolls against targets who have marked HP in the scene.

features.name: Terrifying
features.description: When the Selachi makes a successful attack, all PCs within Close range lose a Hope and you gain a Fear.

features.name: Vicious Bite
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Melee range. On a success, deal 3d8+10 physical damage. When a target uses armor to reduce damage from this attack, they must mark 2 Armor Slots.

features.name: Carve a Path
features.description: Mark a Stress to charge through a group within Close range and make an attack against all targets in the Selachi's path. Targets the Selachi succeeds against take 3d6+8 physical damage and are knocked back to Far range. Selachi Megalodons never stop growing throughout their life, eventually becoming twice the size of most other Selachi. Selachi Warband A Selachi Warband hunts down the Selachi’s foes or conducts raids to claims ships crossing their territory.

name: Selachi Warband
description: A pack of shark-like humanoids.

features.name: Horde
features.description: When the Selachi have marked half or more of their HP, their standard attack deals 3d6+2 physical damage instead. Shiver Their Timbers!: The Selachi deal double damage to PCs with 0 Hope.

features.name: Blood in the Water
features.description: When a PC within Close range of the Selachi marks HP from an ally's attack, you can mark a Stress to immediately spotlight the Warband and make a standard attack against the target. Shadows Shadows are three-dimensional silhouettes of complete darkness except for their glowing eyes. Hungering Darkness The Hungering Darkness is a large shadowy mass that has the instincts and maw of a beast. Animated Shadow An Animated Shadow is a person’s shadow come to life by magic. Only when it is defeated does that person’s shadow return.
features.trigger: When a PC within Close range of the Selachi marks HP from an ally's attack

name: Sheriff
description: A trigger-happy lawman armed with a revolver and badge.

features.name: Tag
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Melee range. On a success, mark a Stress and the Sheriff locks a magical tag around the target's limb. The Sheriff knows the direction of the target while the tag is applied. A PC can make a Finesse Roll (13) to remove the tag. On a failure, the tagged PC takes 2d8 direct magic damage.

features.name: Deliver Shock
features.description: Spend a Fear to force all targets wearing a tag within Far range to make a Strength Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take 2d8 direct magic damage.

features.name: Working the Case
features.description: Long-Term Countdown (6). When the PCs have committed or been framed for a crime, activate the countdown. When it triggers, an arrest warrant is issued and the PCs find wanted posters of themselves. Simiahs Simiahs are ape-folk. Outside their native j their aptitude for climbing, such as lookouts on ships or working in construction. As a Simiah ages a patch of fur on their back turns from bronze to silver to gold. Many Simiahs have hotheaded natures, leading to fierce rivalries within a Simiah community. Simiah Bronzeback Juvenile Simiahs or Bronzebacks are adrenaline junkies who love to climb difficult obstacles or otherwise get close to danger, which can occasionally get them into trouble.

name: Shrieking Stalk
description: A hideous fleshy growth with multiple eyes that creates a blood-curdling cry when creatures approach.

features.name: All-Seeing Eyes
features.description: The Shrieking Stalk is aware of all creatures that are within Far range. Creatures cannot benefit from theHiddencondition as long as the Shrieking Stalk is aware of them.

features.name: Shrill Cry
features.description: Mark a Stressto emit a powerful shriek. All targets within Close range must succeed on a Knowledge Reaction Roll. Targets who fail mark a Stress and you gain a Fear for each. If a target fails by 5 or more, they areVulnerableuntil they clear a Stress. The shriek continues until the Shrieking Stalk is defeated. Allies within Close range of the Stalk gain a +5 to damage.

name: Skeletal Monstrosity
description: A collection of bones that have taken a monstrous humanoid form.

features.name: Bone Breaker
features.description: The Monstrosity's attacks deal direct damage.

features.name: Bone Javelin
features.description: Mark a Stress to pull a bone from the Monstrosity's skeleton and make an attack against a target within Far range. On a success, deal 3d8+l physical damage.

features.name: Rib Cage
features.description: Mark a Stress and make an attack against a target within Very Close range. On a success, the target is Restrained within the Monstrosity until freed with a successful Strength Roll (18). While Restrained, the target can only attack the Monstrosity.

features.name: Augment Body
features.description: Twice per scene, when the Monstrosity is within Very Close range of a pile of bones, they can incorporate them into themselves, clearing a HP and a Stress. Skeleton King The bones of an ancient ruler can rule over undead as their Skeleton King. Restless and unsatisfied with their fate, the Skeleton King and their followers yearn to regain some degree of mortal life. To do this, the Skeleton King can magically steal and wear the skins of mortals.

name: Skeleton King
description: A skeleton monarch ruling over the undead.

features.name: Bone Pile
features.description: If the King is next to a pile of bones, mark a Stress to clear a HP when they act.

features.name: Bone Lord
features.description: Tier 1 Skeleton adversaries within Far range deal double damage.

features.name: Cleaving Creatsword
features.description: Mark a Stress and make an attack against all targets within Melee range. Targets the King succeeds against take 3dlO physical damage.

features.name: Skin Thief
features.description: Spend a Fear to make an attack against a PC within Melee range. On a success, the target's skin is stolen from their body and worn by the King. While wearing the skin, the King loses their "Bone Pile" passive and gains two random cards in the PC's loadout. To use a card feature, when relevant spend or gain Fear instead of Hope. The target becomes permanently Vulnerable until they retrieve their skin with a successful Finesse Roll while within Melee range of the King.

features.name: Long Live the King
features.description: When the King marks their last HP, you can spend a Fear to replace a Skeleton adversary within Far range with the Skeleton King stat block. Roll over any HP or Stress marked. Soul Wardens Soul Wardens are sentient skeletal creatures tasked with ensuring the souls of the dead move on from the Mortal Realm. Ferryman A Ferryman is a guide to souls, escorting them through the Valley of Death to face judgment, then either to the gates of the Hallows Above or the Circles Below.
features.trigger: When the King marks their last HP

name: Small Nesting
description: A small mass of writhing tentacles.

features.name: Grow (Phase Change)
features.description: If there are 3 tokens on the stat block, you can mark a Stress to turn the Nesting into a Medium Nesting. Clear the tokens, but roll over the number of marked HP and Stress onto the new stat block.

features.name: Face Hug
features.description: Make an attack against a target in Very Close range. On a success, the Nesting temporarily attaches to their face, dealing 2dlO direct physical damage. While attached, the target is Vulnerable and must mark a Stress on each PC action roll.

features.name: Cultivate Mass
features.description: When a PC rolls with Fear, you can add the Fear gained to the stat block instead of your Fear pool.
features.trigger: When a PC rolls with Fear

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Nesting makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear, which you can choose to add to the stat block.
features.trigger: When the Nesting makes a successful attack against a PC

name: Sorrow Scourged
description: A person overcome with sorrow, surrounded by sorrow spirits in the form of floating spectral skulls.

features.name: Cloaking Spirits
features.description: Increase the Scourged's Difficulty against attacks by the number of unmarked Stress.

features.name: Counter Emotion
features.description: A PC can make a Presence Roll to display happy behavior. On a success, mark 1d4 Stress.

features.name: Weep for Me
features.description: All PCs within Close range lose a Hope and mark a Stress.

features.name: Wave of Sorrow
features.description: Spend a Fear to spotlight all Sorrow Scourged. All targets within Close range must succeed on a Presence Reaction Roll or take ldl2+10 direct magic damage. Targets within range of two or more Sorrow Scourged who take damage become Vulnerable. Selachi Selachi are shark-folk who live in underwater cities. The Selachi claim to be one of the oldest forms of life, and as such consider anything within the ocean to be their rightful property. This has led to a longstanding feud between the Selachi and Kalimari. Selachi Ghostfin Ghostfins are Selachi who have braved a journey to the abyssal depths of the ocean. They return with a ghostly appearance and flickering form. Selachi Megalodon

name: Soul Flower
description: A horrific amalgam of tentacles, plant life, and alien intentions.

features.name: Eyestalks
features.description: When the Soul Flower enters the scene it has1d4eyestalks. It can have a maximum of six eyestalks.

features.name: Withering Smell
features.description: The first time a target comes within Close range of the Soul Flower they must succeed on an Instinct Reaction Roll. On a failure, the target loses a Hope and you gain a Fear.

features.name: Eye Beam (X)
features.description: Mark X Stressto make an attack against a target within Far range, where X is up to the number of eyestalks the Soulflower has. On a success, a target takes3d12+8magic damage. If a target is hit by more than one beam, combine the damage. The eyestalk then withers and falls away, summoning a Rolling Eye within Melee range.

features.name: Skitter
features.description: Mark a Stressto move up to Far range instead of Close range before making a Tentacle attack.

features.name: Essence Drain
features.description: When the Soul Flower hits a PC with its standard attack, you canspend 2 Fearto drain vital essence from that PC. They roll their Fear die and divide the result by 2 (rounding up). The PC loses that many points from their highest attribute, while the Soul Flower sprouts that many eyestalks from its central mass and clears half the amount of Stress. The PC’s attribute points are restored when the Soul Flower is defeated or the scene ends.
features.trigger: When the Soul Flower hits a PC with its standard attack

name: Sphinx
description: A creature with a lion's body and human head, watching over a desert.

features.name: Flying
features.description: While flying, the Sphinx gains a 4-2 bonus to their Difficulty.

features.name: Hostile Environment
features.description: Mark a Stress to summon 1d4 adversaries from the current Environment, who immediately take the spotlight. Attacks they make while spotlighted this way deal half damage.

features.name: Sands of Time
features.description: Mark a Stress and force a target within Far range to make a Strength Reaction Roll. On a failure, the target ages by 1d6 years. The target can mark Stress to reduce the number of years aged by 1 year per Stress marked.

features.name: Riddle
features.description: Countdown (Loop 3). When the Sphinx is in the spotlight for the first time, pose a riddle to the PCs, change the Environment, and activate the countdown. It ticks down when a PC attacks the Sphinx. A PC can answer the riddle when they are spotlighted, and must mark a Stress on an incorrect answer. When it triggers, change the Environment, pose a new riddle, and remove any adversaries summoned from the previous Environment. If the riddle wasn't answered correctly, each PC loses a Hope and you gain 2 Fear.
features.trigger: Countdown (Loop 3)

features.name: Nature's Karma
features.description: When a creature within Far range uses an area of effect attack or otherwise harms the environment, deal 2d 10 magic damage to the attacker and you gain a Fear. Example Sphinx Riddles Environment	Riddle	Answer Burning Heart of the Woods (SRD)	I am the flame that keeps the heat At the forest’s center, hear my beat	Heart Desert	You might race me, but I can fly I keep watch as the sands slip by	Time Dripping Caverns	A mighty tunneller, I never stop All around us, hear me drop	Water Pitched Battle (SRD)	My twisted words show what to draw Your steely friend who bites with no jaw	Sword Valley of Death	Even kings can’t flee my fate In these lands you’re through my gate	Death Nesting A Nesting is a many-tentacled abomination that feeds on fear, growing in size and forming a smaller Nesting within them. When a Nesting dies, the smaller Nesting emerges from their corpse to continue fighting.
features.trigger: When a creature within Far range uses an area of effect attack or otherwise harms the environment

name: Spikeback Fire-Eater
description: A hairless anteater the size of a horse with spikes all over its body.

features.name: Shredding Claws
features.description: When the Spikeback makes a standard attack, the target must mark an Armor Slot without gaining its benefit (they can still use armor to reduce the damage).

features.name: Whip Tongue
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Close range. On a success, the target takes3d8+2 physical damage and is pulled into Melee range of the Spikeback. They areRestraineduntil they break free with a successful Strength or Finesse Roll. WhileRestrained by the Spikeback, the target loses a Hope each time the Spineback takes the spotlight. If the target cannot lose a Hope, they mark 1 HP.

features.name: Fire Flinger
features.description: Spend a Fearto fling a Cinder Ant at a point within Close range. The Cinder Ant marks 1 HP. All targets within Very Close range of that point must make an Agility Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take3d8+3 direct physical damage and mark 2 Stress. Targets who succeed take half damage. If this defeats the Cinder Ant, it does not trigger its “Ignition End.”

name: Spitfire Rattler
description: A spike-covered snake that hides in brush or sand waiting to strike.

features.name: Brush Viper
features.description: The Rattler has advantage on attacks if they areHidden.

features.name: Spitfire Venom
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Very Close range. On a success, the target takes 3d10+5physical damage. If the target marks HP from the attack they becomeEnvenomeduntil they clear a HP. WhileEnvenomed, the target gains a –2 penalty to attack rolls. This penalty is not cumulative.

features.name: Sidewinder
features.description: If an attack misses the Rattler, mark a Stressto becomeHidden. CINDER ANT
features.trigger: If an attack misses the Rattler

name: Stag Knight
description: Un chevalier aux immenses bois majestueux portant une armure faite d’épines dangereuses.

features.name: From Above
features.description: Quand le Chevalier réussit une attaque standard depuis une position surélevée, il inflige 3d12+3 dégâts physiques au lieu de ses dégâts standard.

features.name: Blade of the Forest
features.description: Faites une attaque contre toutes les cibles à Portée Très Proche. Les cibles touchées subissent des dégâts physiques égaux à 3d4 + le seuil Majeur de la cible.

features.name: Thorny Armor
features.description: Quand le Chevalier subit des dégâts d’une attaque à Portée de Mêlée, infligez 1d10+5 dégâts physiques à l’attaquant.
features.trigger: Quand le chevalier subit des dégâts d’une attaque à portée de mêlée

name: Steel Golem
description: A steel construct etched with runes.

features.name: Ramp Up
features.description: You must spend a Fear to spotlight the Golem. While spotlighted, they can make their standard attack against all targets within range.

features.name: Rune of Magic Protection
features.description: While the Golem has marked 3 or fewer HP, they have immunity to magic damage.

features.name: Steel Body
features.description: The Golem has resistance to physical damage.

features.name: Magnetic field
features.description: Mark a Stress to manipulate all metallic items within Far range. They are either pushed to Far range or pulled to Melee range of the Golem. A PC holding or wearing metal can resist the effect by succeeding on a Strength Reaction Roll.

features.name: Runic Glitch
features.description: When the Golem marks 2 or more HP, they can glitch. Move the Golem to Close range and deal 3d10+8 physical damage to all targets in their path, knocking the targets back to Close range. Gremlin Gremlins are small, mischievous creatures with sharp teeth, large claw-like hands, and dark oily skin that secretes large amounts of grease. Gremlins can magically tinker with objects, and gain enjoyment from breaking things. Ofalthe creatures, Ididnot expect it wouldbe Gremlins that wouldcause the biggestproblem for the trip. These creatures snuckaboard our skyship causing havoc and breaking everything. - Archibald
features.trigger: When the Golem marks 2 or more HP

name: Storm Giant
description: A giant that brings stormy weather and flies upon a bolt of lightning.

features.name: Deadly Flight
features.description: The Giant can move up to Far range instead of Close range before taking an action.

features.name: Lightning
features.description: Spend a Fear and make an attack against all targets in a line up to Far range. Targets you succeed against take 2d8+10 magic damage and become Charged until they succeed on a Finesse Roll (12).

features.name: Summon Storm
features.description: Countdown (Loop 1d6). Mark 2 Stress to change the weather conditions. Activate the countdown, which ticks down each time a PC rolls with Fear or by spending a Fear. When it triggers, all Charged PCs must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll or take 3d6+6 direct magic damage.

features.name: Thunder
features.description: When you succeed on a standard attack, you can mark a Stress to release a loud thunderclap. All targets within Very Close range of that point must make an Instinct Reaction Roll. Targets who fail must mark a Stress and are deafened until the end of the scene. Gnomes Gnomes are small and crafty humanoids, who use their aptitude for science and engineering to defend themselves. Gnome Alchemist Alchemists are proficient with alchemy and can create chemical concoctions to suit their needs. Gnome Gunslinger Gnomes proficient with using black powder can construct weapons that pack an oversized punch. Gnome Tinkerer Tinkerers are proficient in constructing magical objects and devices, such as building a Mecha Suit to defend themselves.
features.trigger: When you succeed on a standard attack

name: Strzyga
description: A restless, half-dead woman cursed with two souls—one buried, one returned. She feeds not just on blood, but on memory, breath, and hope, haunting those who leave the past unfinished.

features.name: Out of Nowhere
features.description: The Strzyga has advantage on attacks if she is Hidden.

features.name: Breath Thief
features.description: Mark a Stress to steal the breath of a creature within Melee range. The target must make a Strength Reaction Roll. On a failure, they lose 1 Hope and cannot speak, or cast spells from domain cards until they mark 2 Stress. The Strzyga then clears a HP. On a success, they mark 1 Stress as their lungs burn with cold emptiness.

features.name: Shadow Leap
features.description: Spend a Fear to disappear from where you are and reappear inside another shadow within Far range. When you reappear, you have resistance to physical damage until you make an attack targeting another creature.

features.name: Split Self
features.description: The Strzyga is not one, but two. Her second soul lingers just beyond the veil. Once per scene, when the Strzyga marks her last HP, spend a Fear: The Strzyga instead vanishes into shadows within Close range and reforms with 1 HP and becomes Hidden. You can spend additional Fear to increase her HP by 2 HP per Fear spend to a maximum of 5 HP.

name: Supersoldier
description: A person with enhanced physical capabilities.

features.name: Enhanced Movement
features.description: Mark a Stress to move to Far range instead of Close range before taking an action.

features.name: Throw
features.description: Mark a Stress to throw a target Restrained by the Supersoldier up to Far range. The target must succeed on an Instinct Reaction Roll (15) or take 1d2O+5 direct physical damage.

features.name: Choke Out
features.description: Spend a Fear to make an attack against a target Restrained by the Supersoldier. On a success, deal 3d12 + 10 direct physical damage, and the target is temporarily Restrained in the Supersoldier's grasp.

features.name: Grapple
features.description: When the Supersoldier succeeds on a standard attack, you can mark a Stress to Restrain the target.
features.trigger: When the Supersoldier succeeds on a standard attack

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Supersoldier makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear. Morphs Morphs are shapeshifters with amorphous bodies that can change form, and spend most of their lives disguised as other creatures or objects. Morph Fully grown Morphs can shapeshift into humanoid forms and are proficient at impersonating others.
features.trigger: When the Supersoldier makes a successful attack against a PC

name: Swarm of Ashen Wasps
description: A swarm of large wasps with wings that burn as they fly.

features.name: Horde (3d4 +3)
features.description: When the Swarm has marked half or more of their HP, their standard attack deals 3d4+3physical damage instead.

features.name: Blinding Ash
features.description: The Swarm can move to occupy the same area as a PC. When it does so, the PC isBlinded until they move away from the Swarm. WhileBlindeda PC isVulnerableand has disadvantage on attack rolls. Additionally, when they move on their spotlight, it is only to a random point within Very Close range at which point they clear all conditions.

name: Swarm Watcher
description: A faerie with multiple sets of eyes all over their body

features.name: Flying
features.description: While flying, the Watcher gains a +2 bonus to their Difficulty.

features.name: All Seeing Eyes
features.description: The Watcher can see through illusions and does not sleep.

features.name: Unsettling Sight
features.description: When the Watcher takes damage from an attack within Far range,mark a Stress to force the attacker to succeed on an Instinct Reaction Roll. On a failure, the attacker marks a Stress and becomesForgetful. WhileForgetful, a PC loses access to an Experience of their choice until their next rest. SCYTHICANT
features.trigger: When the Watcher takes damage from an attack within Far range

name: Terran Gaia
description: A charismatic and revered humanoid made of stone.

features.name: Stoneskin
features.description: The Terran is resistant to physical damage.

features.name: Bone Breaker
features.description: The Terran deals direct damage.

features.name: Study Foe
features.description: Mark a Stress to study a target who has acted in the scene. The Terran gains advantage on attack rolls made against the target until the end of the scene.

features.name: Boulder Roll
features.description: Mark a Stress to tuck into a ball and roll in a line up to Close range. Make an attack against all targets in the Terran's path. Targets the Terran succeeds against take 2d20 physical damage.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Terran makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.
features.trigger: When the Terran makes a successful attack against a PC

name: Terran Scree
description: A humanoid made of stone with quick reflexes.

features.name: Stoneskin
features.description: The Terran is resistant to physical damage.

features.name: Pinball
features.description: Mark a Stress to move up to Close range by bouncing off up to three targets. Make a standard attack against each target the Terran collides with in turn. On a failure, the Scree stops moving and ends their turn.

features.name: Cannonball
features.description: Mark a Stress to move to Far range then make an attack against a target within Melee range. On a success, the target takes 2d12+10 physical damage.

name: Terran Sorcerer
description: A humanoid made of stone with magical abilities.

features.name: Stoneskin
features.description: The Terran is resistant to physical damage.

features.name: Stone Armor
features.description: Mark a Stress to cover the Terran or an ally within Close range in a layer of rock. The target gains a HP, even if it would exceed their HP maximum. A target can only benefit once from this effect.

features.name: Cave-In
features.description: Spend a Fear while in an underground passageway to create a downfall of rocks up to Close range in front of the Terran. All targets must make an Agility Reaction Roll. On a failure, targets take 2dlO physical damage. On a success, targets take half damage. The passageway becomes blocked and can be cleared with a Progress Countdown (8).

name: Terran Tuff

I was stuck in a time loop, the same day repeated over andover. It must have been like this for a while, as some of the beasts looked different to anywhere else I have traveled. Some locals - also trapped - hadgiven these so-caleddinosaurs their names. The carnivorous dinosaurs were among the most terrifying monsters I have ever encountered, and they kiledandate me on numerous occasions, after whichmy day began anew. Eventualy we discoveredthe source of the time anomaly and thankfuly could escape that wretchedplace. - Archibald \ Dragons Dragons are large, winged creatures with elemental breath weapons. Dragons tend to be self-serving narcissists. Some aim to accomplish famous deeds, for example discovering new spells or acquiring power, sometimes even working with adventuring parties to do so (but minimizing any actual risk to themselves). Others accumulate wealth in a hoard, often striking tribute deals with nearby civilizations to do so and relishing the notoriety. Dragons prefer to build their lairs in extreme locations, such as the caldera of a volcano for a fire-breathing dragon. A young dragon will roam far and wide – even to other realms – to find a suitable location, and is reluctant to move from their chosen lair. In their isolation a dragon can grow insane, however, conjuring up wild plots for the sake of notoriety or a more satisfying hoard.

features.trigger: When the Vine Serpent deals damage to a target in Very Close range

name: Void Wraith
description: A shadow being with a floating black hole for a head, that draws in nearby light.

features.name: Bend Space
features.description: You can mark a Stress to teleport up to Far range for movement.

features.name: Staring Into the Void
features.description: When the Wraith makes a successful attack against a PC, they lose a Hope.

features.name: Unravel
features.description: When a target uses armor to reduce damage from the Wraith's attacks, they must mark 2 Armor Slots.

features.name: Black Hole
features.description: Spend a Fear to create a miniature black hole at a point within Far range. All creatures within Close range of that point must make a Strength Reaction Roll. Targets who fail are drawn into Melee range of the black hole and take 2d20 direct magic damage. X Xenn The Xenn are humanoids that inhabit the moon. They have innate psionic abilities that they use to levitate and glide gracefully for movement rather than bounding clumsily in the low gravity. Some Xenn sects are fascinated by the planet that their moon orbits. They have launched expeditions into the Astral Realm to try and identify a shortcut to the planet, returning with tidbits of information from trash banished away by the planet’s magic users. Xenn Astral Raider Astral Raiders venture into the Astral Realm to find information on planet-dwellers and viable routes to their moon’s planet. Unlike most Xenn they arm themselves with weapons in addition to using their psionic powers.

name: Volkolak
description: A cursed wolf-being born of rage and dark magic. Neither man nor beast, the Volkolak stalks the night in search of blood, its eyes gleaming with ancient hatred.

features.name: Blood Frenzy
features.description: For each HP the Volkolak has marked, they deal an additional 1d6 physical damage.

features.name: Dreadhowl
features.description: Mark a Stress to make all targets within Close range lose a Hope. If a target is not able to lose a Hope, they must instead mark 2 Stress.

features.name: Shadow Leap
features.description: Spend a Fear to disappear from where you are and reappear inside another shadow within Far range. When you reappear, you have resistance to physical damage until you make an attack targeting another creature.

features.name: Retaliation
features.description: When the Volkolak takes damage from an attack within Very Close range, you can mark a Stress to make a standard attack against the attacker.

name: Warlord
description: A charismatic and skilled fighter seeking domination, riches, and the ruination of their foes. Adorned with trophies of their bloody conquests and wielding a massive hammer that is always bleeding.

features.name: Relentless (2)
features.description: The Warlord can be spotlighted up to two times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Terrifying
features.description: When the Warlord makes a successful attack, all PCs within Far range lose a Hope and you gain a Fear.

features.name: Raiders
features.description: Spend a Fearto activate a Long-Term Countdown (3) and describe the Warlord’s troops preparing to head out and attack a settlement. When triggered, the raid begins. If not stopped, the raiders set fire to the buildings, steal everything of value, and you gain1d4+1Fear. The Warlord may only have one countdown from this feature active at a time.

features.name: Fight Like You Mean It!
features.description: Spend 2 Fearto activate up to1d4+2allies. Attacks they make while spotlighted this way deal half damage.

features.name: Bloodthirsty Retaliation
features.description: When the Warlord takes damage from within Close range,mark a Stressto have them move into Melee with the attacker and makes a standard attack. On a success, the target also marks a Stress.
features.trigger: When the Warlord takes damage from within Close range

name: Wasp Queen
description: A large demonically infused faerie with wasp-like features and bladed forearms.

features.name: I Am Your Queen
features.description: All allies within Close range of the Queen add +1 to their Difficulty (this does not include the Queen).

features.name: Insectile
features.description: While flying, the Queen canmark a Stressmove up to Far range and can cling to vertical surfaces. If the Queen is not flying, they can only move to Very Close range.

features.name: Alluring Scent
features.description: Spend a Fearto release a burst of pheromones in a Very Close range. Targets in the area must succeed on an Instinct Reaction Roll. Targets who fail become more malleable to the Queen’s words, marking a Stress and becomeEnthralleduntil they break free with a successful Instinct Roll or mark HP. While Enthralled, the target must mark 2 Stress to make an action roll that targets the Queen.

features.name: Summon the Swarm
features.description: Countdown (Loop 1d4). When the Queen is in the spotlight for the first time, activate the countdown. When it triggers, summon a Scythicant Sentinel in Far range that immediately takes the spotlight.
features.trigger: Countdown (Loop 1d4)

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Queen makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.
features.trigger: When the Queen makes a successful attack against a PC

name: Waspwyrm
description: A bloated, flying insect with an unnerving presence.

features.name: Weakening Presence
features.description: PCs within Close range of the Waspwyrm have their thresholds reduced by 5.

features.name: Sticky Spit
features.description: Spend a Fearand spit a sticky glob at a target within Close range. The target must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll or becomeStuckuntil they break free with a successful Strength or Finesse Roll. While Stuck,the target isRestrainedand has disadvantage on attack rolls.

features.name: Infernal Buzzing
features.description: When a PC rolls with Fear, you canmark a Stressto fly above the PC at a Very Close range.
features.trigger: When a PC rolls with Fear

name: Watcher
description: A levitating mushroom creature with a central eye and numerous tentacles and eyestalks protruding from their body.

features.name: Always Watching
features.description: When the Watcher makes an attack, the target's Evasion is halved against the attack.

features.name: Toxic Spores
features.description: Spend a Fear to force all targets within Very Close range to make an Instinct Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take 2d8+4 magic damage and become Poisoned until they clear HP. While Poisoned they have disadvantage on action rolls.

features.name: Penetrating Gaze
features.description: When a PC uses an Armor Slot against the Watcher's attack, you can mark a Stress to force the target to mark an additional HP.
features.trigger: When a PC uses an Armor Slot against the Watcher's attack

features.name: Tentacle Grab
features.description: When a target moves out of Very Close range of the Watcher, you can make an attack against them. On a success, the target is Restrained in the Watcher's tentacle until they break free with a successful Finesse or Strength Roll.
features.trigger: When a target moves out of Very Close range of the Watcher

name: Weredragon
description: A hybrid dragon humanoid with draconic wings.

features.name: Deadly Flight
features.description: The Weredragon can move up to Far range instead of Close range before making an action roll.

features.name: Silver Vulnerability
features.description: The Weredragon is Vulnerable against silver coated weapons.

features.name: Beastly Power
features.description: When you initially roll damage, if you roll the maximum value on a die, also roll a new die of that type and add it to the total.

features.name: Elemental Breath
features.description: Mark a Stress and make an attack against all targets within Very Close range in front of the Weredragon. On a success, deal 2d6+10 magic damage.

features.name: Infectious Bite
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Melee range. On a success, deal 3d8+3 physical damage. If the target marks HP, you can spend a Fear to start a Lycanthropy Countdown (3). Tick down the countdown after each long rest. When it triggers, the target is inflicted with a Curse of Lycanthropy (Weredragon form) and gains the Lycan ancestry (this book). Weredragon Weredragons take the form of a humanoid dragon, and much like dragons are driven by power and greed. Unlike Drakona the Weredragon has draconic wings. Wererat Wererats take the form of humanoid rats. Wererats are cunning Lycans who use their new rat-form to command swarms of rats, acting as their rat king or rat queen.

name: Weretiger
description: A hybrid tiger humanoid with orange striped fur.

features.name: Silver Vulnerability
features.description: The Weretiger is Vulnerable against silver coated weapons.

features.name: Beastly Power
features.description: When you initially roll damage, if you roll the maximum value on a die, also roll a new die of that type and add it to the total.

features.name: Pounce
features.description: Mark a Stress to leap to a point within Close range and make an attack with advantage against all targets within Melee range of that point. Targets the Weretiger succeeds against take 3d6+4 physical damage.

features.name: Infectious Bite
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Melee range. On a success, deal 3d8+l physical damage. If the target marks HP, you can spend a Fear to start a Lycanthropy Countdown (3). Tick down the countdown after each long rest. When it triggers, the target is inflicted with a Curse of Lycanthropy (Weretiger form) and gains the Lycan ancestry (this book). Werewolf Werewolves take the form of humanoid wolves. Werewolves use brute power and heightened senses to hunt their prey with power heightened during a full moon.

name: Whale
description: A huge mammal with flippers and tail.

features.name: Sing
features.description: Mark a Stress to sing a calming melody. All targets within Far range and outside of the Whale must succeed on an Instinct Reaction Roll or fall Asleep until they take damage, they hear a loud sound, or they mark 2 Stress to wake.

features.name: Swallow
features.description: Make an attack against a target. On a success, spend a Fear and the target is Swallowed by the Whale. While Swallowed, the target is Restrained and must mark a Stress when they act. If the Whale takes Major or greater damage, all Swallowed creatures are released, taking ldlO+3 physical damage and moving to Very Close range of the Whale. C Canids Canids are dog-like humanoids who are extremely loyal to their allies, to the extent that they can sacrifice themselves for others. Canid Bodyguard A Canid Bodyguard has dedicated their life to protect a Master. Canid Rover

name: Whisper Wraith
description: The echoes of broken promises and dreams unfulfilled, these wraiths appear as floating, translucent shrouds of tattered black velvet in a vaguely humanoid shape.

features.name: Spectral
features.description: The Wraith is immune to physical damage and weak against magic damage. Additionally, the Wraith can move through solid objects.

features.name: Spooky
features.description: When the Wraith makes a successful attack, all PCs within Close range must mark a Stress and you gain a Fear.

features.name: Nightmare Shroud
features.description: Spend a Fear to have the Wraith wrap themself around a PC within Melee range, making them Shrouded until they or an ally succeed on a Strength or Attack Roll (20) to dislodge the Wraith. If it's an attack roll, the Shrouded PC takes the damage. While Shrouded, a PC has disadvantage on all attacks and marks a Stress when they roll with Fear.

name: Wight
description: A sentient corpse of a person since brought back to life.

features.name: Unshakeable Resolve
features.description: The first time the Wight would mark their last HP, don't mark it.

features.name: Death Touch
features.description: When a target marks HP from the Wight's attack, their maximum HP is reduced by the same amount until their next rest.

features.name: Grave Danger
features.description: Undead allies within Very Close range gain a +5 bonus to damage dealt.

features.name: Raise Dead
features.description: Mark a Stress to summon 1d4 Tier 1 or 2 Skeleton or Zombie adversaries from the ground.

features.name: Soul Hunger
features.description: Spend a Fear and make an attack against a PC within Close range. On a success, the PC's soul is removed from their body. The PC's body becomes a Shambling Zombie (SRD) under your control and the PC's soul uses the Ghost stat block with no marked HP or Stress (they continue to roll Duality Dice and use their proficiency for damage). The PC can use the Ghost's "Possess" action to reclaim their body if the Shambling Zombie is either still alive or was defeated no more than 24 hours prior. Winter Winter is a colossal ice elemental, whose appearance is rumored to permanently coat the land in snow, create extreme weather and bring an endless night.

name: Winged Basilisk
description: A winged creature with a blue striped body and a deadly gaze.

features.name: Deadly Flight
features.description: While flying, the Basilisk can move up to Far range instead of Close range before taking an action.

features.name: Easy Pickings
features.description: Targets of the Basilisk's attacks must state if they are looking at the Basilisk. The Basilisk has advantage against targets who avoid looking at the Basilisk.

features.name: Lightning Breath
features.description: Mark a Stress and make an attack against all targets in a line up to Far range. On a success, a target takes 2d 10+5 magic damage. Targets with an active vaporization countdown take double damage.

features.name: Vaporizing Gaze
features.description: When the Basilisk takes damage from an attacker within Very Close range, or makes a standard attack against a target that is looking at them, you can spend a Fear to force the target to make an Instinct Reaction Roll. On a failure, they begin to turn to cloud, marking a HP and starting a Vaporization Countdown (4). The countdown ticks down when the Basilisk is attacked. When it triggers, the target must make a death move. If the Basilisk is defeated, all vaporization countdowns end. Battle Pyramid The Battle Pyramid is a geometric construct covered with a runic marking on each face, similar to the more powerful Battle Box (SRD). The runes transform randomly into different forms of attack. These arcane creations explode after their defeat, revealing precious loot housed within them. The Battle Pyramid appeared after we witnessed someone get stabbed by a dagger in the heart. -	Eryn
features.trigger: When the Basilisk takes damage from an attacker within Very Close range

name: Woods Witch
description: An elderly crone with slightly feral appearance.

features.name: Coven
features.description: Increase Difficulty by 1 for each Hag within Far range.

features.name: Call Upon Nature
features.description: Mark a Stress to animate 2d4 trees within Far range, forming Minor Treant (SRD) Minions.

features.name: Polymorph
features.description: Mark a Stress to force a PC to make an Instinct Reaction Roll. On a failure, the target turns into a tree with their face outlined in the bark. As a tree, they can mark a Stress to animate and use the Treant (SRD) stat block. They can revert to their true form by succeeding on an Instinct Roll or if the Witch is defeated.

features.name: Curse of the Wilds
features.description: Long-Term Countdown (1d6). When the Witch marks their last HP, you can spend a Fear to curse the PCs with retribution from Mother Nature. Activate the countdown. When it triggers, 1d4+2 animal adversaries attack. Halflings Halflings are small but plucky humanoids said to have unnatural amounts of luck that helps them survive in a world much larger than them. Halfling Sneak Halfling Sneaks are rogues that utilize their small size and stealth. They often work together in pairs or small groups. Halfling Underlord A Halfling Underlord is a charismatic leader of a secret faction vying for power, such as a group of rebel fighters or leader of a thieves’ guild. Two Halflings in a Trenchcoat Two Halfling Sneaks - one on top of the other’s shoulders - can work together to create an alter ego.

name: Wyvern
description: A draconic creature with a poisonous tail, often used as a mount.

features.name: Flyby
features.description: While flying, the Wyvern moves up to Close range after making a standard attack.

features.name: Tail Sting
features.description: Make an attack using the Wyvern's tail. On a success, spend a Fear to deal 1d20 physical damage and Poison the target until their next rest or they succeed on a Knowledge Roll (17). While Poisoned, the target must roll a d6 before they make an action roll. On a result of 2 or lower, they must mark a HP. Drakona Drakona are humanoids with dragon ancestors, giving them an affinity to one of the elements. Societies of Drakona often have a communal aim to become notorious, much like their dragon ancestors, and work collectively towards this audacious goal. Drakona Berserker Berserkers fight as mercenaries and in front of crowds for fame and gold.

name: Xeralyth Concubine
description: A Xeralyth Dragoon who has caught the attention of the Queen and grown in size and strength.

features.name: Favor
features.description: When the Concubine enters the scene, place 6 favor tokens on their stat block. The Concubine gains a bonus to damage rolls equal to the number of favor tokens on their stat block. When the Concubine succeeds on an attack, add a token. When the Concubine fails on an attack, or marks HP, remove a token. If the Concubine loses its last token, replace it with a Xeralyth Dragoon of equal HP (if 6 or less).

features.name: Phase Lunge
features.description: Mark a Stressto make an attack against a target within Far range. On a success, the target takes3d12+2physical damage and you gain a Fear.

features.name: Sweeping Strike
features.description: Spend a Fearto make an attack against all targets within Very Close range. Targets the Concubine succeeds against take3d12+2physical damage and are knocked back to Far range.

features.name: Interpose
features.description: When the Xeralyth Queen is within Close range and would take damage,mark a Stressto take the damage instead. Instead of losing a favor token, the Concubine gains one.
features.trigger: When the Xeralyth Queen is within Close range and would take damage

features.name: Maelstrom
features.description: When a PC moves within Close range of the Concubine,mark a Stressto force them to make a Strength Reaction Roll. On a failure, they are pulled into Melee range of the Concubine andRestrained until they break free with a successful Strength roll.
features.trigger: When a PC moves within Close range of the Concubine

name: Xeralyth Dragoon
description: Spiky, tentacled guard who phases across planes, seeking and destroying in service to their queen.

features.name: Royal Proximity
features.description: While within Very Close range of the Xeralyth Queen, the Dragoon gains a +2 bonus to their Difficulty and a +1 bonus to their attack rolls.

features.name: Phase Lunge
features.description: Mark a Stressto make an attack against a target within Far range. On a success, the target takes3d8+6physical damage and isVulnerableuntil their next action.

features.name: Interpose
features.description: When the Xeralyth Queen is within Close range and would take damage,mark a Stressto take the damage instead.
features.trigger: When the Xeralyth Queen is within Close range and would take damage

name: Xeralyth Queen
description: An astral, jellyfish-like monarch with shimmering psychic membranes, who shifts across planes to prey and conquer.

features.name: Broodmother’s Will
features.description: When the Queen is spotlighted, you can also spotlight a Xeralyth Dragoon within Close range.

features.name: Psychic Lance
features.description: Mark a Stressto choose a target within Very Far range. That target must make a Presence Reaction Roll. On a failure, the target takes3d12+6magic damage and you gain a Fear. On a success, the target marks 2 Stress.

features.name: Psychic Scream
features.description: Spend a Fearto unleash a mental shriek. All PCs in Very Far range must succeed on an Instinct Reaction Roll. On a failure, they lose 1 Hope; if they have no Hope, they mark 1 HP.

features.name: Displacement
features.description: When the Queen takes damage from an attack outside Close range, you canspend a Fearto distort space and teleport the attacker next to the Queen. She then makes an attack against them.
features.trigger: When the Queen takes damage from an attack outside Close range

features.name: Crushing Embrace
features.description: When the Queen succeeds on a standard attack,mark a StresstoRestrainthe target until they break free with a successful Strength Roll.
features.trigger: When the Queen succeeds on a standard attack

name: Xolothi Mage
description: An axolotl-like humanoid wielding a wooden staff.

features.name: Regenerating Limbs
features.description: When the Xolothi takes damage, you can mark a Stress and lose a limb instead of marking HP. Put an Aether token onto this stat block. The Xolothi must have at least one arm to make their standard attack and one leg to move. The limb regrows in 1d4 days.

features.name: Energy Bolts
features.description: Spend an Aether token and make an attack against two targets within Far range. On a success, deal 1d2 magic damage. If the bolts hit a single target combine the damage.

features.name: Magic Resistance
features.description: When the Xolothi takes magic damage you can spend an Aether token to take half damage. Xolothi Shaman Xolothi Shamans are the most attuned to the aether and can conduct corruption cleansing ceremonies to quickly regrow limbs. Xolothi Warrior Warriors can sacrifice a limb in combat to enhance their fighting powers.
features.trigger: When the Xolothi takes magic damage you can spend an Aether token to take half damage

name: Xolothi Shaman
description: An axolotl-like humanoid shimmering with energy from the aether. Motives & Tactics: Cleanse corruption, channel energy, sacrifice

features.name: Regenerating Limbs
features.description: When the Xolothi takes damage, you can mark a Stress and lose a limb instead of marking HP. Put an Aether token onto this stat block. A spectral limb appears and the limb regrows in 1d4 days.

features.name: Aether Cloud
features.description: When the Xolothi has at least one Aether token, attacks that deal magic damage to the Xolothi trigger a wild magic effect (Introduction of this book).

features.name: Energy Boost
features.description: Spend an Aether token to spotlight two other Xolothi within Far range. Attacks they make while spotlighted in this way deal half damage.

features.name: Energy Blast
features.description: Spend an Aether token to unleash energy in a line up to Close range. All targets must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll or take 3d8+2 magic damage. Y Yeti Yetis are large humanoid beasts who live in arctic regions. They are versatile hunters who can use their innate strength, stealth, and magical abilities to track, incapacitate, and take out their targets.

name: Xolothi Warrior
description: An axolotl-like humanoid armed with a sword and shield.

features.name: Regenerating Limbs
features.description: When the Xolothi takes damage, you can mark a Stress and lose a limb instead of marking HP. Put an Aether token onto this stat block. The Xolothi must have at least one arm to make their standard attack and one leg to move. The limb regrows in 1d4 days.

features.name: Energized
features.description: The Xolothi can be spotlighted by spending an Aether token instead of a Fear.

features.name: Sever Limb
features.description: Mark a Stress and lose a limb. Put two Aether tokens on this stat block.

features.name: Energized Blow
features.description: When the Xolothi succeeds with a standard attack, you can spend an Aether token to increase the damage dealt by IdlO.
features.trigger: When the Xolothi succeeds with a standard attack

name: Young Ice Dragon
description: Un dragon bleu glacier aux quatre membres puissants et aux ailes givrées.

features.name: Relentless (3)
features.description: Le Dragon peut être mis sous le projecteur jusqu’à trois fois par tour du MJ. Dépensez une Peur comme d’habitude pour le placer sous le projecteur.

features.name: Rend and Crush
features.description: Si une cible endommagée par le Dragon ne coche pas un Emplacement d’armure pour réduire les dégâts, elle doit cocher un Stress.

features.name: No Hope
features.description: Quand un PJ obtient Peur à Portée Loin du Dragon, il perd un Espoir.

features.name: Blizzard Breath
features.description: Libérez un tourbillon glacé devant le Dragon à Portée Proche. Toutes les cibles doivent faire un jet de Réaction d’Agilité. Les cibles qui échouent subissent 4d6+5 dégâts magiques et sont Entravées par la glace jusqu’à un jet de Force réussi. Les cibles qui réussissent doivent cocher 2 Stress ou subir la moitié des dégâts.

features.name: Avalanche
features.description: Déchaînez une immense chute de neige et de glace couvrant toutes les autres créatures à Portée Loin. Toutes les cibles doivent réussir un jet de Réaction d’Instinct ou être ensevelies, devenant Vulnérables jusqu’à ce qu’elles se dégagent. Pour chaque PJ qui échoue, vous gagnez une Peur.

features.name: Frozen Scales
features.description: Quand une créature réussit une attaque contre le Dragon depuis Portée Très Proche, elle doit cocher un Stress et devenir Glacée jusqu’à son prochain repos ou qu’elle retire un Stress. Tant que Glacée, elle a un désavantage sur les jets d’attaque.
features.trigger: Quand une créature réussit une attaque contre le dragon depuis portée très proche

features.name: Momentum
features.description: Quand le Dragon réussit une attaque contre un PJ, vous gagnez une Peur.
features.trigger: Quand le dragon réussit une attaque contre un pj, vous gagnez une peur

name: Zaxian
description: A flying octopus-like creature with tentacles and bat wings.

features.name: Flying
features.description: While flying, the Zaxian gains a +2 bonus to their Difficulty.

features.name: Shift Reality
features.description: Switch the battlefield between two and three dimensions. All other creatures must mark a Stress.

features.name: Strangle and Dangle
features.description: Make a standard attack against a target within Very Close range. On a success, mark a Stress to deal 2d12+4 physical damage as the Zaxian's tentacles strangle the target by the neck. The target is Restrained until they break free with a successful Finesse or Strength Roll or the Zaxian takes Severe damage. While Restrained in this way, the target must mark a Stress on each PC action roll.

features.name: Dimensional Squeeze
features.description: When the battlefield is in two dimensions, spend a Fear to flatten the remaining depth, affecting all creatures within Close range. Targets must succeed on a Strength Reaction Roll or take 2d20 magic damage.

features.name: Humanoid Shield
features.description: When the Zaxian has Restrained a target, spend a Fear to put them in the way of an attack from Very Close range or further that succeeds against the Zaxian. The Restrained target takes the damage instead of the Zaxian. Zombies Zombies are mindless undead creatures brought to life by necromantic magic. Their skin decays on their bones and they shamble towards the living, with only the half-formed thought of eating brains driving them forwards. Zombie Beast Zombie Beasts have rotting bodies and honed instincts for hunting down prey.
features.trigger: When the Zaxian has Restrained a target

name: Zhar-Ptitsa
description: A radiant firebird from distant legends, the Zhar-Ptitsa blazes with beauty and doom alike. Its feathers shine with otherworldly light, offering fortune—or cursing those who seek to claim it.

features.name: Relentless (3)
features.description: The Zhar-Ptitsa can be spotlighted up to three times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Flying
features.description: While flying, the Zhar-Ptitsa gains a +2 bonus to their Difficulty.

features.name: Ash and Light
features.description: Spend a Fear and choose a point within Far range and let the Zhar-Ptitsa's feathers rain down on within Close range of it. All targets must make an Agility Reaction Roll. If they fail, they take 2d10 magic damage. If they succeed, they must mark a Stress. All allies of the Zhar-Ptitsa in this area clear a Stress and are cleansed of one condition.

features.name: Curse of the Firebird
features.description: Choose a target within Far range and temporarily Curse them. While the target is Cursed, you can mark a Stress when that target rolls with Hope to make the roll be with Fear instead.

features.name: Shriek of Twilight
features.description: Countdown (Loop 1d6). When the Zhar-Ptitsa takes damage for the first time, activate the countdown. When it triggers, the Zhar-Ptitsa lets loose a soul-touching shriek. All targets in up to Close range must make an Instinct Reaction Roll or be overwhelmed by memories of failure. Targets previously touched by the feathers of Ash and Light have disadvantage on this roll. Targets who fail lose 2 Hope and roll with disadvantage on all action rolls until they roll a success with Hope.

features.name: Molten Wings
features.description: When a creature within Melee range deals damage to the Zhar-Ptitsa, they take 1d10 magic damage.
